_meta.purpose: Source-of-truth metric definitions for future knowledge-graph ingestion. NOT a graph itself (no synthetic nodes / roll-up edges); each metric lists its code-grounded input dependencies.
_meta.generated_from: unique_metrics_catalog.csv (325 LLM-deduped metrics) + per-metric full-codebase dependency derivation (LLM subagents)

field_notes.depends_on: metric_ids and/or input_ids this metric is computed from (code-grounded, sub-platform-specific)
field_notes.aliases: duplicate metric ids folded into this canonical metric during dedup
field_notes.input_nodes: raw source columns + ML model-output intermediates that depends_on references; documentation only

blended.active_ads.title: Active Ads
blended.active_ads.formula_human: Count of distinct AD_IDs with IMPRESSIONS > 0, summed across Google (MART_GOOGLE_AD_PERFORMANCE) and Meta (MART_META_AD_PERFORMANCE) ad-level marts.
blended.active_ads.formula_explanation: Number of unique ads that served at least one impression during the period across Google and Meta. An ad with zero impressions is not counted as active.
blended.active_ads.description: Number of unique ads that served at least one impression during the period across Google and Meta. Ads with zero impressions are excluded.

blended.active_audiences.title: Active Audiences
blended.active_audiences.formula_human: Count of distinct AUDIENCE_IDs present in MART_AUDIENCE_PERFORMANCE over the period (Google Ads + Meta Ads audience rows combined).
blended.active_audiences.formula_explanation: Count of distinct audiences that recorded activity during the period across Google and Meta. Each unique audience ID with data in the date range is counted once.
blended.active_audiences.description: Count of distinct audiences with activity (rows) during the period across Google and Meta. Each unique audience ID in the date range is counted once.

blended.active_campaigns.title: Active Campaigns
blended.active_campaigns.formula_human: COUNT(DISTINCT CAMPAIGN_ID) in MART_CAMPAIGN_MATRIX where SPEND>0 OR CONVERSION_VALUE>0 OR IMPRESSIONS>0 OR CLICKS>0, over the period (Google + Meta; Klaviyo counted separately in context).
blended.active_campaigns.formula_explanation: Total count of distinct campaigns with activity in the period across Google Ads, Meta Ads, and Klaviyo. Only campaigns with spend, revenue, impressions, or clicks are counted.
blended.active_campaigns.source_code_ref: backend/app/repositories/campaign_matrix.py:281-368 (_fetch_period_summary: COUNT(DISTINCT CAMPAIGN_ID), WHERE SPEND>0 OR CONVERSION_VALUE>0 OR IMPRESSIONS>0 OR CLICKS>0), :601-631 (_build_active_campaigns_metric)
blended.active_campaigns.description: Total count of distinct campaigns with any activity in the period across Google Ads and Meta Ads (Klaviyo shown in the context split). Only campaigns with spend, revenue, impressions, or clicks are counted.

blended.audience_conversions.title: Audience Conversions
blended.audience_conversions.formula_human: SUM(CONVERSIONS) over MART_AUDIENCE_PERFORMANCE for the period (Google + Meta audience-targeted rows); total attributed revenue SUM(CONVERSION_VALUE) carried as context.
blended.audience_conversions.formula_explanation: Total conversions attributed to audience-targeted campaigns across Google and Meta for the period. Total attributed revenue (SUM of CONVERSION_VALUE) is carried alongside as supporting context.
blended.audience_conversions.description: Total conversions attributed to audience-targeted campaigns across Google and Meta for the period. Attributed revenue (SUM of CONVERSION_VALUE) is carried alongside as context.

blended.budget_at_risk.title: Budget at Risk
blended.budget_at_risk.formula_human: SUM(SPEND) of campaigns classified into the Optimize/Cut quadrant (SPEND >= median SPEND AND ROAS < median ROAS); % of Total = budget_at_risk / total_spend * 100. Quadrant ROAS = CONVERSION_VALUE/SPEND per campaign; medians taken over campaigns with positive spend and ROAS.
blended.budget_at_risk.formula_explanation: The dollar amount of spend sitting in the Optimize/Cut quadrant (high spend, low ROAS). This is budget being spent inefficiently that could be reallocated.
blended.budget_at_risk.description: The dollar amount of spend sitting in the Optimize/Cut quadrant (high spend, low ROAS) — budget being spent inefficiently that could be reallocated.

blended.budget_exhaustion_days.title: Days Until Budget Exhaustion
blended.budget_exhaustion_days.formula_human: Days Until Exhaustion = (monthly budget - MTD spend) / daily run rate, where monthly budget = SUM(MAX DAILY_BUDGET per campaign) * days-in-month and daily run rate = AVG of daily SUM(DAILY_SPEND) over the period. DAILY_SPEND is Google Ads + Meta Ads spend from MART_BUDGET_PACING.
blended.budget_exhaustion_days.description: Estimated number of days until the monthly ad budget is exhausted at the current daily spend run rate.

blended.cac.title: Blended CAC
blended.cac.formula_human: Blended CAC = total ad spend (SUM(SPEND) from MART_MARKETING_DAILY, Google + Meta) / new customers (SUM(NEW_CUSTOMERS) from MART_CUSTOMER_ACQUISITION).
blended.cac.formula_explanation: Blended Customer Acquisition Cost divides all paid marketing spend across every channel by the total number of new customers acquired in the period. It shows the true all-in average cost to win one customer, regardless of which channel they came from.
blended.cac.source_code_ref: backend/app/repositories/customer_acquisition.py:659-691 (get_blended_cac_current: SUM(SPEND) from MART_MARKETING_DAILY / SUM(NEW_CUSTOMERS) from MART_CUSTOMER_ACQUISITION)
blended.cac.description: Blended Customer Acquisition Cost: all paid marketing spend across channels divided by total new customers acquired in the period. The all-in average cost to win one customer regardless of channel.

blended.cac_efficiency.title: CAC Efficiency
blended.cac_efficiency.formula_human: CAC Efficiency = first-purchase AOV / blended CAC. first_purchase_aov = SUM(FIRST_ORDER_REVENUE)/new_customers; blended CAC = total ad spend / new customers. A ratio above 1 means the first order alone recoups acquisition cost.
blended.cac_efficiency.formula_explanation: CAC Efficiency divides the average first-order value by the blended cost to acquire a customer. A ratio above 1 means a customer's first purchase already covers more than what it cost to acquire them; below 1 means the first order does not yet pay back acquisition spend.
blended.cac_efficiency.description: Average first-order value divided by the blended cost to acquire a customer. Above 1 means a customer's first purchase covers more than acquisition cost; below 1 means the first order does not yet pay back acquisition spend.

blended.conversion_rate.title: Site Conversion Rate
blended.conversion_rate.formula_human: Site Conversion Rate = SUM(TOTAL_ORDERS) / SUM(SESSIONS) * 100 over the period (orders from Magento, sessions from GA4, both via MART_CEO_DAILY_PULSE); Change % vs comparison period.
blended.conversion_rate.formula_explanation: Percentage of website sessions that result in a purchase. This measures overall site effectiveness at converting visitors to customers. Ecommerce benchmarks typically range from 2-4%, but seed/gardening businesses often achieve higher rates.
blended.conversion_rate.description: Percentage of website sessions that result in a purchase — overall site effectiveness at converting visitors to customers.

blended.conversions.title: Total Conversions
blended.conversions.formula_human: Total Conversions = SUM(PLATFORM_CONVERSIONS) from MART_MARKETING_MIX (platform-reported, Google + Meta).
blended.conversions.formula_explanation: The total number of conversions reported by the ad platforms (Google + Meta) for the selected period. These are platform self-reported and feed the Average CPA calculation.
blended.conversions.description: Total number of platform-reported conversions across Google + Meta for the period. Platform self-reported; feeds the Average CPA calculation.

blended.cpa.title: Average CPA
blended.cpa.formula_human: Average CPA = (Google spend + Meta spend) / SUM(PLATFORM_CONVERSIONS), all from MART_MARKETING_MIX. Lower is better; trend is inverted on the card.
blended.cpa.formula_explanation: Average Cost Per Acquisition is total paid spend divided by total platform-reported conversions. Lower is better. Note the trend is inverted on the card: a rising CPA is flagged as a negative trend.
blended.cpa.description: Average Cost Per Acquisition: total paid spend divided by total platform-reported conversions. A rising CPA is flagged as a negative trend.

blended.ctr.title: Blended CTR
blended.ctr.formula_human: Blended CTR = SUM(CLICKS) / SUM(IMPRESSIONS) * 100, impression-weighted across Google (MART_GOOGLE_AD_PERFORMANCE) and Meta (MART_META_AD_PERFORMANCE).
blended.ctr.formula_explanation: Impression-weighted average click-through rate across Google and Meta creatives. Because it weights by impressions, high-volume ads dominate the figure rather than a simple average of per-ad CTRs.
blended.ctr.description: Impression-weighted average click-through rate across Google and Meta creatives. High-volume ads dominate the figure rather than a simple per-ad average.

blended.ga4_magento_gap.title: GA4 vs Magento Gap
blended.ga4_magento_gap.formula_human: GA4 vs Magento Gap = total GA4 revenue (SUM TOTAL_REVENUE) - Magento store revenue (SUM MAGENTO_REVENUE); pre-computed daily as GA4_MAGENTO_GAP and summed. Gap % = gap / Magento revenue * 100.
blended.ga4_magento_gap.formula_explanation: The difference between GA4-reported total revenue and actual Magento store revenue, with the percentage taken relative to Magento revenue. A positive gap means GA4 over-reports versus the store's actual sales of record.
blended.ga4_magento_gap.description: Difference between GA4-reported total revenue and actual Magento store revenue, with percentage relative to Magento revenue. A positive gap means GA4 over-reports versus the store's actual sales of record.

blended.google_attribution_gap.title: Google Attribution Gap
blended.google_attribution_gap.formula_human: Google Attribution Gap = GOOGLE_PLATFORM_REVENUE - GOOGLE_GA4_REVENUE (summed daily GOOGLE_GAP) from MART_ATTRIBUTION_DAILY. Gap % = gap / GA4 Google revenue * 100.
blended.google_attribution_gap.formula_explanation: The dollar difference between what Google Ads self-reports as revenue and what GA4 attributes to Google. The gap percentage is expressed relative to GA4 Google revenue. A positive gap means Google over-reports versus GA4 last-click, driven by view-through, cross-device, and wider attribution windows.
blended.google_attribution_gap.description: Dollar difference between Google Ads self-reported revenue and GA4-attributed Google revenue. A positive gap means Google over-reports versus GA4 last-click (view-through, cross-device, wider attribution windows).

blended.klaviyo_attribution_gap.title: Klaviyo Attribution Gap
blended.klaviyo_attribution_gap.formula_human: Klaviyo Attribution Gap = Klaviyo self-reported revenue - GA4 email revenue; pre-computed daily as KLAVIYO_GAP and summed. EMAIL_REVENUE = KLAVIYO_SELF_REPORTED_REVENUE - KLAVIYO_GAP. Gap % = gap / GA4 email revenue * 100.
blended.klaviyo_attribution_gap.formula_explanation: The difference between revenue Klaviyo claims for email/SMS and the email revenue GA4 attributes. The daily gap is pre-computed in the mart and summed; the percentage is relative to GA4 email revenue. A positive gap means Klaviyo over-reports email's contribution versus GA4.
blended.klaviyo_attribution_gap.description: Difference between revenue Klaviyo claims for email/SMS and the email revenue GA4 attributes. A positive gap means Klaviyo over-reports email's contribution versus GA4.

blended.mer.title: Marketing Efficiency Ratio (MER)
blended.mer.formula_human: MER = total Magento store revenue / total ad spend (Google + Meta), 0 when spend is 0. On marketing-mix: MAGENTO_REVENUE (MART_FUNNEL_DAILY) / (GOOGLE_SPEND + META_SPEND) (MART_MARKETING_MIX); on monthly-review: NET_REVENUE / TOTAL_AD_SPEND (MART_CEO_DAILY_PULSE).
blended.mer.formula_explanation: Marketing Efficiency Ratio measures total store revenue (Magento, the source of truth) against total ad spend. Unlike ROAS, which only counts paid-attributed revenue, MER captures the holistic impact of marketing on the whole business including organic and direct halo effects.
blended.mer.description: Marketing Efficiency Ratio: total store revenue (Magento, source of truth) against total ad spend. Unlike ROAS (paid-attributed revenue only), MER captures holistic marketing impact including organic and direct halo effects.

blended.meta_attribution_gap.title: Meta Attribution Gap
blended.meta_attribution_gap.formula_human: Meta Attribution Gap = META_PLATFORM_REVENUE - META_GA4_REVENUE (summed daily META_GAP) from MART_ATTRIBUTION_DAILY. Gap % = gap / GA4 Meta revenue * 100.
blended.meta_attribution_gap.formula_explanation: The dollar difference between Meta (Facebook/Instagram) self-reported revenue and GA4-attributed Meta revenue, with the gap percentage expressed relative to GA4 Meta revenue. Meta typically shows larger gaps than Google because of its 7-day-click / 1-day-view attribution windows that GA4 last-click does not capture.
blended.meta_attribution_gap.description: Dollar difference between Meta self-reported revenue and GA4-attributed Meta revenue. Meta typically shows larger gaps than Google due to 7-day-click / 1-day-view windows GA4 last-click does not capture.

blended.month_end_revenue_projection.title: Month End Revenue Projection
blended.month_end_revenue_projection.formula_human: Projected Month-End Revenue = MTD revenue + (avg daily revenue from selected period * days left in month). Revenue = SUM(CONVERSION_VALUE) from MART_BUDGET_PACING (Google + Meta ad-platform revenue).
blended.month_end_revenue_projection.description: Projected total advertising-attributed revenue at month end, extrapolating month-to-date revenue plus the average daily revenue applied to remaining days.

blended.month_end_spend_projection.title: Month End Spend Projection
blended.month_end_spend_projection.formula_human: Projected Month-End Spend = MTD spend + (avg daily spend from selected period * days left in month). DAILY_SPEND = Google Ads + Meta Ads spend from MART_BUDGET_PACING. Weighted variant splits weekday/weekend.
blended.month_end_spend_projection.description: Projected total ad spend at month end, extrapolating month-to-date spend plus the average daily spend applied to remaining days in the month.

blended.optimize_cut_campaigns.title: Optimize/Cut Campaigns
blended.optimize_cut_campaigns.formula_human: Count of campaigns with SPEND >= median SPEND AND ROAS < median ROAS (per-campaign ROAS = SUM(CONVERSION_VALUE)/SUM(SPEND) in MART_CAMPAIGN_MATRIX; medians over campaigns with positive spend and ROAS).
blended.optimize_cut_campaigns.formula_explanation: Counts campaigns in the Optimize/Cut quadrant (High spend, low ROAS). Campaigns spending heavily with poor returns - they require immediate optimization or budget reduction. The card also shows that quadrant's total spend and average ROAS.
blended.optimize_cut_campaigns.description: Count of campaigns in the Optimize/Cut quadrant (high spend, low ROAS) — spending heavily with poor returns, needing immediate optimization or budget reduction. Card also shows quadrant total spend and avg ROAS.

blended.purchase_reconciliation.title: Purchase Reconciliation
blended.purchase_reconciliation.formula_human: Purchase Reconciliation Delta = GA4 purchases (SUM PURCHASES) - Magento orders (SUM MAGENTO_ORDERS) from MART_FUNNEL_DAILY; Gap % = delta / Magento orders * 100.
blended.purchase_reconciliation.formula_explanation: Measures the gap between GA4-tracked purchases and Magento orders for the period. A non-zero delta flags tracking discrepancies between analytics and the order system.
blended.purchase_reconciliation.description: Gap between GA4-tracked purchases and Magento orders for the period. A non-zero delta flags tracking discrepancies between analytics and the order system.

blended.revenue_reconciliation.title: Revenue Reconciliation
blended.revenue_reconciliation.formula_human: Headline = total Magento store revenue (source of truth). Platform total = Google platform revenue + Meta platform revenue; GA4 total = SUM(TOTAL_REVENUE). Gaps: (platform - Magento)/Magento*100 and (GA4 - Magento)/Magento*100 (attribution); marketing-mix variant uses MART_MARKETING_MIX PLATFORM_CONVERSION_VALUE + MART_FUNNEL_DAILY Magento + GA4 paid revenue.
blended.revenue_reconciliation.formula_explanation: Reconciles three revenue views: platform self-reported revenue, GA4-attributed paid revenue, and actual Magento store revenue. Magento is treated as the source of truth, and the gap percentages quantify how much platform and GA4 figures over- or under-state real revenue.
blended.revenue_reconciliation.description: Reconciles three revenue views: combined platform self-reported revenue (Google + Meta), GA4 total/attributed revenue, and actual Magento revenue. Magento is the source of truth; the gaps quantify how platform and GA4 figures over- or under-state real revenue.

blended.roas.title: Blended ROAS
blended.roas.formula_human: Blended ROAS = total ad-platform conversion value / total ad spend (Google + Meta), spend-weighted, 0 when spend is 0. marketing-mix uses GA4 paid revenue (MART_CRO_SOURCE_MEDIUM_DAILY) over Google+Meta spend; campaign-matrix/weekly-exec/quarterly use SUM(CONVERSION_VALUE)/SUM(SPEND); ceo-pulse/monthly use NET_REVENUE/TOTAL_AD_SPEND. An 'actual ROAS' variant uses Magento revenue.
blended.roas.formula_explanation: Return on Ad Spend measures advertising efficiency. A 4x ROAS means $4 revenue for every $1 spent. This blends Google and Meta performance into a single efficiency metric.
blended.roas.description: Blended return on ad spend: total revenue divided by total paid spend across channels. Numerator is platform-reported conversion value (or GA4 paid / Magento revenue depending on dashboard); denominator is blended ad spend.

blended.scale_campaigns.title: Scale Campaigns
blended.scale_campaigns.formula_human: Count of campaigns with SPEND >= median SPEND AND ROAS >= median ROAS (per-campaign ROAS = SUM(CONVERSION_VALUE)/SUM(SPEND) in MART_CAMPAIGN_MATRIX; medians over campaigns with positive spend and ROAS).
blended.scale_campaigns.formula_explanation: Counts campaigns in the Scale quadrant (High spend, high ROAS). Campaigns that are both high-spending and high-performing - proven winners that are prime candidates for additional budget. The card also shows that quadrant's total spend and average ROAS.
blended.scale_campaigns.description: Count of campaigns in the Scale quadrant (high spend, high ROAS) — proven winners that are prime candidates for additional budget. Card also shows quadrant total spend and avg ROAS.

blended.scale_opportunity_campaigns.title: Scale Opportunity Campaigns
blended.scale_opportunity_campaigns.formula_human: Count of campaigns with SPEND < median SPEND AND ROAS >= median ROAS (also includes campaigns with zero spend but positive revenue); per-campaign ROAS = SUM(CONVERSION_VALUE)/SUM(SPEND); medians over campaigns with positive spend and ROAS.
blended.scale_opportunity_campaigns.formula_explanation: Counts campaigns in the Scale Opportunity quadrant (Low spend, high ROAS). Efficient but under-invested campaigns - high ROAS with low spend, representing untapped growth potential. The card also shows that quadrant's total spend and average ROAS.
blended.scale_opportunity_campaigns.source_code_ref: backend/app/repositories/campaign_matrix.py:312-345 (with_quadrants: spend=0 AND revenue>0 OR spend<median AND roas>=median -> 'Scale Opportunity'), :750-810 (_build_quadrant_metrics)
blended.scale_opportunity_campaigns.description: Count of campaigns in the Scale Opportunity quadrant (low spend, high ROAS) — efficient but under-invested, representing untapped growth potential. Card also shows quadrant total spend and avg ROAS.

blended.test_kill_campaigns.title: Test/Kill Campaigns
blended.test_kill_campaigns.formula_human: Count of campaigns with SPEND < median SPEND AND ROAS < median ROAS (per-campaign ROAS = SUM(CONVERSION_VALUE)/SUM(SPEND) in MART_CAMPAIGN_MATRIX; medians over campaigns with positive spend and ROAS).
blended.test_kill_campaigns.formula_explanation: Counts campaigns in the Test/Kill quadrant (Low spend, low ROAS). Campaigns with both low spend and low ROAS - either early tests not yet optimized or underperformers to pause. The card also shows that quadrant's total spend and average ROAS.
blended.test_kill_campaigns.description: Count of campaigns in the Test/Kill quadrant (low spend, low ROAS) — either early tests not yet optimized or underperformers to pause. Card also shows quadrant total spend and avg ROAS.

blended.total_ad_spend.title: Total Ad Spend
blended.total_ad_spend.formula_human: Total Ad Spend = Google Ads spend + Meta Ads spend for the period (excludes Klaviyo). marketing-mix sums GOOGLE_SPEND + META_SPEND from MART_MARKETING_MIX; ceo-pulse/monthly use TOTAL_AD_SPEND (=GOOGLE_SPEND+META_SPEND) from MART_CEO_DAILY_PULSE; creative-performance sums SPEND across the Google and Meta ad-level marts. Google costs converted from micros.
blended.total_ad_spend.formula_explanation: Combined advertising spend across Google Ads and Meta Ads platforms. Google costs are stored in micros (millionths of currency) and converted. This represents your total paid media investment.
blended.total_ad_spend.description: Combined advertising spend across Google Ads and Meta Ads — total paid media investment. This is the account-level blend that other blended ratios (ROAS, MER, CPA, CAC) divide by.

blended.yoy_revenue_change.title: YoY Revenue Change
blended.yoy_revenue_change.formula_human: YoY Change % = (most recent completed year revenue - prior year revenue) / prior year revenue * 100. Revenue is actual Magento net revenue (SUM(net_revenue) from MART_CEO_DAILY_PULSE by year); MART_ANNUAL_PLANNING_SUMMARY.annual_revenue used in the legacy per-metric path.
blended.yoy_revenue_change.formula_explanation: Year-over-year revenue change, comparing the most recent completed year's annual revenue to the year before it from the planning summary mart. Positive values indicate growth; negative values indicate decline.
blended.yoy_revenue_change.source_code_ref: backend/app/repositories/annual_planning.py:1234-1315 (get_kpis: actual_revenue from MART_CEO_DAILY_PULSE NET_REVENUE, yoy_change), :233-255 (_build_yoy_change_metric); legacy get_yoy_change_metric:573-630 uses MART_ANNUAL_PLANNING_SUMMARY.annual_revenue
blended.yoy_revenue_change.description: Year-over-year revenue change, comparing the most recent completed year's revenue to the year before. Positive values indicate growth; negative values indicate decline.

derived.estimated_orders.title: Estimated Orders
derived.estimated_orders.formula_human: Weekly Orders (estimated) = weekly revenue / 50 (assumed $50 average order value); WoW Change % = revenue week-over-week. Weekly revenue sourced from MART_CHANNEL_REVENUE_DAILY / FCT_DAILY_REVENUE in the weekly-exec scorecard.
derived.estimated_orders.formula_explanation: Estimated order count based on revenue divided by a typical average order value. This provides a volume metric to complement revenue totals.
derived.estimated_orders.description: Estimated order count = revenue divided by a typical $50 AOV. A volume proxy to complement revenue totals; not a true order count.

derived.gross_margin.title: Gross Margin
derived.gross_margin.formula_human: Gross Margin % (estimated) = (NET_REVENUE * 0.50 - TOTAL_AD_SPEND) / NET_REVENUE * 100, assuming 50% COGS. From MART_CEO_DAILY_PULSE.
derived.gross_margin.formula_explanation: Estimated gross margin percentage for the selected period, assuming a 50% COGS benchmark on NET_REVENUE less total ad spend, over revenue. Computed in SQL against MART_CEO_DAILY_PULSE. Because true COGS is not available, this is an estimate.
derived.gross_margin.description: Estimated gross margin %: 50% COGS benchmark on net revenue, less total ad spend, over revenue. Because true COGS is not available, this is an estimate.

derived.ltv_cac_ratio.title: LTV:CAC Ratio
derived.ltv_cac_ratio.formula_human: LTV:CAC = LTV / CAC, where LTV = (NET_REVENUE / TOTAL_ORDERS) * 1.5 and CAC = TOTAL_AD_SPEND / NEW_USERS. All from MART_CEO_DAILY_PULSE. A ratio of 3x+ is healthy.
derived.ltv_cac_ratio.formula_explanation: Ratio of estimated Customer Lifetime Value to Customer Acquisition Cost for the period. LTV is AOV (revenue / orders) times a 1.5 repeat-purchase multiplier; CAC is total ad spend divided by NEW_USERS. Computed in SQL/Python against MART_CEO_DAILY_PULSE. A ratio of 3x or more is considered healthy.
derived.ltv_cac_ratio.description: Ratio of estimated Customer Lifetime Value to Customer Acquisition Cost. LTV is AOV (revenue/orders) times a 1.5 repeat-purchase multiplier; CAC is total ad spend / NEW_USERS.

derived.projected_revenue.title: Projected Revenue
derived.projected_revenue.formula_human: Projected Revenue = (Google budget * Google ROAS) + (Meta budget * Meta ROAS) + (Email budget * Email ROAS), where budget split is 52% Google / 46% Meta / 2% Email of total budget, channel ROAS are prior-year actuals (annual_roas from MART_ANNUAL_PLANNING_SUMMARY), and Email ROAS fixed at 25x. When actual Magento revenue exists it is used directly.
derived.projected_revenue.formula_explanation: Expected annual revenue derived from the budget split across channels multiplied by each channel's historical ROAS. The default channel mix is 52% Google / 46% Meta / 2% Email, and prior-year channel ROAS values drive the projection (Email ROAS fixed at 25x). Change is measured against prior-year revenue.
derived.projected_revenue.description: Expected annual revenue from the budget split across channels multiplied by each channel's historical ROAS. Default mix 52/46/2; prior-year channel ROAS drive the projection (Email fixed at 25x).

derived.revenue_per_session.title: Revenue per Session
derived.revenue_per_session.formula_human: Revenue per Session = SUM(REVENUE) / SUM(SESSIONS) over the period, from MART_FUNNEL_DAILY (CRO dashboard 'rpu' metric).
derived.revenue_per_session.formula_explanation: Average revenue generated per session, dividing revenue by sessions. Despite the 'per user' shorthand, the backend computes this per session - it blends conversion rate and AOV into one efficiency number.
derived.revenue_per_session.description: Average revenue generated per session (revenue / sessions). Despite the 'per user' shorthand, the backend computes this per session, blending conversion rate and AOV into one efficiency number.

ga4.avg_product_price.title: Average Item Price
ga4.avg_product_price.formula_human: Average Item Price = SUM(ITEM_REVENUE) / SUM(ITEMS_PURCHASED) over the period (product-level on MART_GA4_PRODUCT_DAILY; level-3 category-level on MART_GA4_CATEGORY_DAILY)
ga4.avg_product_price.description: Average selling price per unit, computed as total item revenue divided by total units purchased over the period. Product-overview computes it from MART_GA4_PRODUCT_DAILY at item granularity; product-category computes the same ratio from MART_GA4_CATEGORY_DAILY filtered to category_level=3. Reflects realized price including mix and promotions, not list price.

ga4.avg_revenue_per_new_product.title: Avg Revenue Per New Product
ga4.avg_revenue_per_new_product.formula_human: Avg Revenue Per New Product = SUM(ITEM_REVENUE) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT ITEM_ID), 0) across new products, each counted from its launch date through date_to
ga4.avg_revenue_per_new_product.formula_explanation: Total revenue from newly launched products divided by the count of distinct new products, giving the average revenue each new product has generated since its launch.
ga4.avg_revenue_per_new_product.description: Total revenue from newly launched products divided by the count of distinct new products. New products are those whose first-ever activity date (MIN(DATE_DAY) over MART_GA4_PRODUCT_ID_DAILY) falls within the launch window, excluding placeholder IDs '(not set)' and '(other)'. Revenue is summed from each product's launch date through date_to.

ga4.bounce_rate.title: Bounce Rate
ga4.bounce_rate.formula_human: Bounce Rate = 1 - (SUM(ENGAGED_SESSIONS) / SUM(SESSIONS)), a weighted ratio across the period
ga4.bounce_rate.formula_explanation: Share of sessions that were not engaged, computed as one minus the engaged-session ratio. It is a weighted average across the period, not a simple mean of daily rates.
ga4.bounce_rate.description: Share of sessions that were not engaged, computed as one minus the engaged-session ratio. It is a weighted average over the period (1 - SUM(ENGAGED_SESSIONS)/SUM(SESSIONS)) from MART_WEBSITE_PERFORMANCE, not a simple mean of the daily BOUNCE_RATE column (which is used only for the sparkline). Inverse of engagement_rate.

ga4.cart_abandonment_rate.title: Cart Abandonment Rate
ga4.cart_abandonment_rate.formula_explanation: Share of add-to-cart sessions that did not result in a purchase, from the funnel mart. It quantifies how much demand is lost between cart and checkout.
ga4.cart_abandonment_rate.description: Share of add-to-cart sessions that did not result in a purchase, from MART_FUNNEL_DAILY. PURCHASES here are GA4 unique purchasers (funnel-consistent). Quantifies demand lost between cart and checkout.

ga4.cart_to_purchase_rate.title: Cart-to-Purchase Rate
ga4.cart_to_purchase_rate.formula_human: Cart-to-Purchase Rate = AVG over qualifying products of (ITEMS_PURCHASED / ITEMS_ADDED_TO_CART) * 100, over last 30 days, limited to items with 50-10000 cart adds and >0 purchases
ga4.cart_to_purchase_rate.formula_explanation: The benchmark conversion rate from cart add to completed purchase across products with meaningful demand. It is computed as the average of each qualifying product's own purchased-over-added ratio, expressed as a percent, limited to items with 50 to 10000 cart adds and at least one purchase so outliers and noise do not skew the benchmark.
ga4.cart_to_purchase_rate.description: Benchmark conversion from cart-add to purchase across products with meaningful demand. Computed as the average of each qualifying product's own purchased/added ratio (AVG(ITEMS_PURCHASED::FLOAT/ITEMS_ADDED_TO_CART)*100) on MART_GA4_PRODUCT_DAILY, limited to items with 50<adds<10000 and purchases>0 so outliers do not skew the benchmark.

ga4.checkout_completion_rate.title: Checkout Completion Rate
ga4.checkout_completion_rate.formula_explanation: Share of begun checkouts that completed in a purchase, from the funnel mart. It isolates friction inside the checkout flow itself.
ga4.checkout_completion_rate.description: Share of begun checkouts that completed in a purchase, from MART_FUNNEL_DAILY. PURCHASES are GA4 unique purchasers (funnel-consistent). Isolates friction inside the checkout flow.

ga4.declining_items.title: Declining Items
ga4.declining_items.formula_human: Declining Items = COUNT(DISTINCT ITEM_NAME) where this_week SUM(ITEMS_PURCHASED) < 0.5 * four_weeks_ago SUM(ITEMS_PURCHASED) AND four_weeks_ago > 20, over a fixed trailing 60-day window anchored to CURRENT_DATE()
ga4.declining_items.formula_explanation: Counts products whose weekly sales have collapsed to less than half of what they were four weeks ago. Only items that had a meaningful base (more than 20 purchases four weeks ago) qualify, so the metric flags real momentum loss rather than normal small-number noise. It intentionally uses a fixed 60-day window anchored to today so the week-over-week comparison always has data, regardless of the dashboard date filter.
ga4.declining_items.description: Counts products whose current-week units purchased fell below half of their level four weeks earlier, among items with >20 purchases four weeks ago. Uses weekly DATE_TRUNC buckets of ITEMS_PURCHASED on MART_GA4_PRODUCT_DAILY over a fixed 60-day window anchored to CURRENT_DATE() (intentionally ignores the dashboard date filter so the WoW comparison always has data).

ga4.direct_revenue_share.title: Direct Traffic Share
ga4.direct_revenue_share.description: Percentage of total GA4 revenue attributed to Direct traffic. Computed from MART_ATTRIBUTION_DAILY as the pre-aggregated DIRECT_REVENUE column over TOTAL_REVENUE (all channels). High Direct share signals tracking gaps, untagged campaigns, or dark-social traffic. DIRECT_REVENUE is summed internally but is not exposed as its own user-facing metric (emitted here as raw field ga4.direct_revenue).

ga4.engagement_rate.title: Engagement Rate
ga4.engagement_rate.formula_human: Engagement Rate = SUM(ENGAGED_SESSIONS) / SUM(SESSIONS), a weighted ratio across the period
ga4.engagement_rate.formula_explanation: Share of sessions that were engaged, computed as engaged sessions over total sessions. It is a weighted average across the period and is the inverse of bounce rate.
ga4.engagement_rate.description: Share of sessions that were engaged, computed as SUM(ENGAGED_SESSIONS)/SUM(SESSIONS) from MART_WEBSITE_PERFORMANCE (weighted average across the period, not a mean of the daily ENGAGEMENT_RATE column). Inverse of bounce_rate.

ga4.estimated_conversion_rate.title: Conversion Rate (estimated)
ga4.estimated_conversion_rate.formula_human: Conversion Rate (estimated) = (current_engaged / current_sessions) * 100 * 0.1 (engaged-session proxy with 0.1 scaling factor)
ga4.estimated_conversion_rate.description: An APPROXIMATION of conversion rate on the weekly-exec dashboard, computed in build_kpi_summary as engaged_sessions / sessions * 100 * 0.1 using engaged sessions as a proxy for conversions with a 0.1 scaling factor. It is NOT a true purchases/sessions rate. Sessions and engaged_sessions come from MART_CHANNEL_REVENUE_DAILY (SCORECARD_QUERY). Distinct from ga4.session_conversion_rate (true purchases/sessions).

ga4.estimated_lost_revenue.title: Est. Lost Revenue
ga4.estimated_lost_revenue.formula_human: Estimated Lost Revenue = SUM over items with adds>50 of (bench_conv - COALESCE(ITEMS_PURCHASED/ITEMS_ADDED_TO_CART, 0)) * ITEMS_ADDED_TO_CART * COALESCE(ITEM_REVENUE/ITEMS_PURCHASED, avg_price), last 30 days; bench_conv = AVG(ITEMS_PURCHASED/ITEMS_ADDED_TO_CART) across items with adds>50 and purchases>0; avg_price = AVG(ITEM_REVENUE/ITEMS_PURCHASED) fallback
ga4.estimated_lost_revenue.formula_explanation: Estimates the revenue you are leaving on the table because under-converting items are not selling at the benchmark rate. For each item with real cart demand, it takes the gap between the catalog-wide average conversion rate and the item's own conversion rate, multiplies by its cart adds to get the missed purchases, then multiplies by the item's price to convert that into dollars.
ga4.estimated_lost_revenue.description: Revenue left on the table because under-converting items are not selling at the catalog benchmark rate. For each item with >50 cart adds it takes the gap between the catalog-wide average conversion (benchmark) and the item's own ITEMS_PURCHASED/ITEMS_ADDED_TO_CART, multiplies by cart adds (missed purchases) then by item price (ITEM_REVENUE/ITEMS_PURCHASED, falling back to catalog avg price). All from MART_GA4_PRODUCT_DAILY. The benchmark and avg price are computed inline (same logic as ga4.cart_to_purchase_rate and ga4.avg_product_price).

ga4.funnel_drop_rate.title: Funnel Drop Rate
ga4.funnel_drop_rate.formula_human: Funnel Drop Rate = (1 - SUM(EVENT_COUNT WHERE EVENT_NAME='purchase') / NULLIF(SUM(EVENT_COUNT WHERE EVENT_NAME='add_to_cart'), 0)) * 100, sitewide over last 30 days
ga4.funnel_drop_rate.formula_explanation: The percentage of add-to-cart events that never reach a purchase event over the past 30 days. It is one minus the ratio of purchase events to add-to-cart events, expressed as a percent. Unlike the cart-to-purchase rate (which is a product-level benchmark from product daily data), this is a sitewide event-funnel measure drawn from the GA4 events mart.
ga4.funnel_drop_rate.description: Percentage of add-to-cart events that never reach a purchase event over the past 30 days. One minus the ratio of purchase EVENT_COUNT to add_to_cart EVENT_COUNT from MART_GA4_EVENTS_DAILY (event-level funnel, sitewide). Distinct from ga4.cart_to_purchase_rate which is a product-level benchmark from MART_GA4_PRODUCT_DAILY.

ga4.items_at_risk.title: Items At Risk
ga4.items_at_risk.formula_human: Items At Risk = COUNT(DISTINCT ITEM_NAME) HAVING SUM(ITEMS_PURCHASED) = 0 AND SUM(ITEMS_ADDED_TO_CART) > 50 (with ITEMS_ADDED_TO_CART < 10000 cap), last 30 days
ga4.items_at_risk.formula_explanation: Counts products that customers added to their cart but nobody actually purchased in the past 30 days. The threshold of more than 50 cart adds filters out incidental activity so only items with real, repeated purchase intent are flagged. Zero purchases despite genuine cart demand is a strong signal of a stockout, broken checkout, or pricing problem on that item.
ga4.items_at_risk.description: Counts products customers added to cart but nobody purchased in the past 30 days, with >50 cart adds (and an <10000 anomaly cap) so only items with real repeated purchase intent are flagged. Grouped by ITEM_NAME on MART_GA4_PRODUCT_DAILY. Zero purchases despite genuine cart demand signals a stockout, broken checkout, or pricing problem.

ga4.new_products_count.title: New Products Launched
ga4.new_products_count.formula_human: New Products Launched = COUNT(DISTINCT ITEM_ID) HAVING MIN(DATE_DAY) >= launch_since_date, excluding ITEM_ID IN ('(not set)','(other)')
ga4.new_products_count.formula_explanation: Count of distinct products whose first-ever appearance in the data falls within the selected launch window. Each product is counted as launched on the first date it shows any activity.
ga4.new_products_count.description: Count of distinct products whose first-ever appearance (MIN(DATE_DAY) over MART_GA4_PRODUCT_ID_DAILY) falls within the launch window. Each product is counted as launched on its earliest activity date; placeholder IDs '(not set)' and '(other)' are excluded.

ga4.new_products_revenue.title: New Products Revenue
ga4.new_products_revenue.formula_human: New Products Revenue = SUM(ITEM_REVENUE) across products whose MIN(DATE_DAY) is in the launch window, summed from each product's launch date through date_to
ga4.new_products_revenue.formula_explanation: Total revenue generated by all products that launched within the selected window, summed across their activity from launch through the end date.
ga4.new_products_revenue.description: Total revenue generated by all products that launched within the selected window, summed across their activity from launch through date_to. Joins new-product ITEM_IDs (first appearance in window, placeholders excluded) back to MART_GA4_PRODUCT_ID_DAILY and sums ITEM_REVENUE. Scoped to new products only, so it is distinct from the all-product/all-traffic ga4.revenue node.

ga4.new_products_success_rate.title: New Product Success Rate
ga4.new_products_success_rate.formula_human: Success Rate = SUM(CASE WHEN per-product SUM(ITEM_REVENUE) > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) * 100 across new products in the window
ga4.new_products_success_rate.formula_explanation: Percentage of newly launched products that generated any revenue (revenue > 0) since launch, out of all new products in the window. Measures the share of launches that achieved at least minimal commercial traction.
ga4.new_products_success_rate.description: Percentage of newly launched products that generated any revenue (revenue > 0) since launch, out of all new products in the window. Each new product's total ITEM_REVENUE is computed from launch date through date_to on MART_GA4_PRODUCT_ID_DAILY; the rate is the share with positive revenue.

ga4.paid_revenue.title: Total Paid Revenue
ga4.paid_revenue.formula_human: Total Paid Revenue = SUM(NVL(REVENUE, 0)) WHERE SESSION_MEDIUM IN ('cpc','paid')
ga4.paid_revenue.description: Sum of GA4 revenue for all sessions whose medium is cpc or paid, from MART_CRO_SOURCE_MEDIUM_DAILY filtered by SESSION_MEDIUM IN ('cpc','paid'). Implemented under the code metric id 'conversion_value' on the marketing-mix dashboard. A paid-traffic-only filtered aggregate, distinct from the all-traffic ga4.revenue node.

ga4.product_conversion_rate.title: Product Conversion Rate
ga4.product_conversion_rate.formula_explanation: Measures conversion efficiency from product view to purchase. For a seed company with high-intent traffic, rates of 15-25% are typical. Low rates may indicate pricing issues, poor imagery, or catalog navigation problems.
ga4.product_conversion_rate.description: Conversion efficiency from product view to purchase: units purchased over units viewed, as a percent. Product-overview computes it from MART_GA4_PRODUCT_DAILY at item granularity; product-category computes the same ratio from MART_GA4_CATEGORY_DAILY filtered to category_level=3 (purchase_rate).

ga4.product_revenue.title: Product Revenue
ga4.product_revenue.formula_human: Product Revenue = SUM(ITEM_REVENUE) over the period
ga4.product_revenue.description: Total product (item) revenue. Product-overview sums ITEM_REVENUE from MART_GA4_PRODUCT_DAILY at item granularity; product-category sums ITEM_REVENUE from MART_GA4_CATEGORY_DAILY at category_level=3 (level-3 category revenue). Product-scoped item revenue, distinct from the all-traffic GA4 ga4.revenue node.

ga4.revenue.title: Total GA4 Revenue
ga4.revenue.formula_human: Total GA4 Revenue = SUM(TOTAL_REVENUE) across all traffic sources and channels (no channel filter)
ga4.revenue.formula_explanation: Total revenue from all channels this week based on GA4 attribution. Revenue is attributed at the session level using the default channel grouping from Google Analytics.
ga4.revenue.description: Sum of all GA4-tracked revenue across every traffic source and channel (paid, organic, direct, referral, email, etc.). On attribution it sums the pre-aggregated TOTAL_REVENUE column of MART_ATTRIBUTION_DAILY; on weekly-exec it sums REVENUE from MART_CHANNEL_REVENUE_DAILY. The all-traffic baseline against which channel attribution gaps and direct share are measured. Distinct from ga4.paid_revenue (paid-only) and ga4.product_revenue (item-level).

ga4.session_conversion_rate.title: Conversion Rate
ga4.session_conversion_rate.formula_human: Conversion Rate = SUM(PURCHASES) * 100 / NULLIF(SUM(SESSIONS), 0), where PURCHASES = GA4 unique purchasers
ga4.session_conversion_rate.description: Overall site conversion rate: percentage of sessions that ended in a purchase, computed as PURCHASES/SESSIONS*100 from MART_FUNNEL_DAILY. PURCHASES are GA4 unique purchasers for funnel consistency. Used on both cro and funnel dashboards (identical formula and source). A true purchases/sessions rate, distinct from ga4.estimated_conversion_rate (engaged-session 0.1 proxy on weekly-exec).

ga4.sessions.title: Sessions
ga4.sessions.formula_explanation: Total website sessions from all traffic sources including organic, paid, direct, email, and referral. Sessions measure top-of-funnel traffic volume.
ga4.sessions.description: Total website sessions from all traffic sources (organic, paid, direct, email, referral) over the period. On website-performance it sums SESSIONS from MART_WEBSITE_PERFORMANCE; on weekly-exec it sums SESSIONS from MART_CHANNEL_REVENUE_DAILY (scorecard). Top-of-funnel traffic-volume metric.

ga4.true_oos_count.title: True OOS Count
ga4.true_oos_count.formula_human: True OOS Count = COUNT(DISTINCT ITEM_NAME) HAVING SUM(ITEMS_PURCHASED) = 0 AND SUM(ITEMS_ADDED_TO_CART) > 100 (with ITEMS_ADDED_TO_CART < 10000 cap), last 30 days
ga4.true_oos_count.formula_explanation: A high-confidence count of products that are effectively out of stock based on customer behavior. It uses a stricter bar than Items At Risk (more than 100 cart adds versus more than 50) so that only items with very strong, sustained demand and zero sales are counted. These are your most certain stockouts where many customers tried to buy and none succeeded.
ga4.true_oos_count.description: High-confidence count of products effectively out of stock based on behavior: zero ITEMS_PURCHASED with >100 ITEMS_ADDED_TO_CART (anomaly cap <10000) over 30 days on MART_GA4_PRODUCT_DAILY. Uses a stricter bar than ga4.items_at_risk (>100 adds vs >50) so only items with very strong sustained demand and zero sales are counted.

ga4.units_sold.title: Units Sold
ga4.units_sold.formula_human: Units Sold = SUM(ITEMS_PURCHASED) over the period
ga4.units_sold.formula_explanation: Counts total units purchased across all categories. This volume metric helps understand demand patterns independent of price changes and is critical for inventory planning and fulfillment capacity.
ga4.units_sold.description: Total product units purchased across all products in the period. Product-overview sums ITEMS_PURCHASED from MART_GA4_PRODUCT_DAILY at item granularity; product-category sums ITEMS_PURCHASED (items_sold) from MART_GA4_CATEGORY_DAILY at category_level=3. A pure volume measure independent of price, useful for demand and inventory planning.

ga4.users.title: Users
ga4.users.formula_explanation: Total unique users who visited over the period, summed from the website performance mart (GA4 sourced). It measures audience reach.
ga4.users.description: Total unique users who visited over the period, summed as SUM(USERS) from MART_WEBSITE_PERFORMANCE (GA4 sourced). Measures audience reach.

google_ads.below_top_impression_rate.title: Below Top Position Rate
google_ads.below_top_impression_rate.formula_human: Below Top Position % = 100 − Top Position Rate
google_ads.below_top_impression_rate.formula_explanation: Percentage of impressions that did NOT land in top position (i.e. below organic results), computed as 100 minus the impression-weighted top-position rate from MART_COMPETITIVE_INTELLIGENCE. Lower is better — it indicates stronger ad placement. Note: the backend metric id is below_top; the absence of true impression-share data means this is a top-position proxy, not a budget-lost-impression-share figure.
google_ads.below_top_impression_rate.description: Percentage of Google Ads impressions that did NOT land in top position (above organic results), computed as 100 minus the impression-weighted top-position rate from MART_COMPETITIVE_INTELLIGENCE. A top-position proxy (true impression-share data is unavailable); lower is better.

google_ads.clicks.title: Clicks
google_ads.clicks.formula_explanation: Total clicks on Google Ads in the selected date range, summed from the campaign-performance mart. Each click represents a user engaging with an ad.
google_ads.clicks.description: Total clicks on Google Ads across all campaign types in the selected date range, summed from MART_GOOGLE_CAMPAIGN_PERFORMANCE. Each click is a user engaging with an ad.

google_ads.conversions.title: Conversions
google_ads.conversions.formula_explanation: Total conversions attributed to Google Ads campaigns in the selected date range, summed from the campaign-performance mart. Includes all conversion actions configured in the account.
google_ads.conversions.description: Total conversions attributed to Google Ads campaigns (all types) in the selected date range, summed from MART_GOOGLE_CAMPAIGN_PERFORMANCE. Includes all configured conversion actions.

google_ads.cpa.title: CPA
google_ads.cpa.formula_explanation: Cost per acquisition - total spend divided by total conversions for the period. Shows the average cost to generate one conversion across all Google Ads campaigns.
google_ads.cpa.description: Cost per acquisition for Google Ads (all campaign types): total spend divided by total conversions over the selected period. Lower is better.

google_ads.impressions.title: Impressions
google_ads.impressions.formula_explanation: Total number of times your ads were shown across all campaigns in the selected period, summed from MART_COMPETITIVE_INTELLIGENCE. Higher impressions indicate greater visibility and reach in the market.
google_ads.impressions.description: Total ad impressions for Google Ads in the selected date range. On the google-overview dashboard this is SUM(IMPRESSIONS) from MART_GOOGLE_CAMPAIGN_PERFORMANCE; on the competitive dashboard the same node is SUM(IMPRESSIONS) from MART_COMPETITIVE_INTELLIGENCE. Each impression is one display of an ad.

google_ads.position_gap_rate.title: Position Gap Rate
google_ads.position_gap_rate.formula_human: Position Gap % = Top Position Rate − Absolute Top Rate
google_ads.position_gap_rate.formula_explanation: The gap between the top-position rate and the absolute-top (#1) rate, computed from MART_COMPETITIVE_INTELLIGENCE. It represents impressions that appeared above organic results but not in the #1 spot — the opportunity to climb from 'above organic' to the top slot. Note: the backend metric id is position_gap; this is a top-position proxy, not a rank-lost-impression-share figure.
google_ads.position_gap_rate.description: The impression-weighted gap between the top-position rate and the absolute-top (#1) rate, from MART_COMPETITIVE_INTELLIGENCE. Represents impressions above organic results but not in the #1 slot — the opportunity to climb to the top. A top-position proxy, not rank-lost impression share.

google_ads.roas.title: ROAS
google_ads.roas.formula_explanation: Return on ad spend across all Google Ads campaigns - total conversion value divided by total spend for the period. A ROAS of 5.0 means $5 of revenue for every $1 spent.
google_ads.roas.description: Return on ad spend for Google Ads (all campaign types): total conversion value divided by total spend over the selected period, from MART_GOOGLE_CAMPAIGN_PERFORMANCE. A ROAS of 5.0 means $5 revenue per $1 spent.

google_ads.spend.title: Spend
google_ads.spend.formula_explanation: Total Google Ads spend across all campaigns in the selected date range, summed directly from the campaign-performance mart (values already in dollars).
google_ads.spend.description: Total Google Ads spend across all campaign types in the selected date range, summed directly from MART_GOOGLE_CAMPAIGN_PERFORMANCE (values already in dollars).

google_ads.top_impression_rate.title: Top Position Rate
google_ads.top_impression_rate.formula_human: Top Position Rate = SUM(TOP_POSITION_PCT × IMPRESSIONS) ÷ SUM(IMPRESSIONS)
google_ads.top_impression_rate.description: Impression-weighted percentage of Google Ads impressions in top position (above organic results), from MART_COMPETITIVE_INTELLIGENCE. Computed as the impression-weighted average of TOP_POSITION_PCT; higher indicates stronger ad placement.

google_pmax.conversion_value.title: Performance Max Conversion Value
google_pmax.conversion_value.description: Total conversion value (revenue) attributed to Performance Max campaigns over the selected period, summed from MART_GOOGLE_CAMPAIGN_PERFORMANCE filtered to CAMPAIGN_TYPE='PERFORMANCE_MAX'. This is the numerator used for PMax ROAS.

google_pmax.conversions.title: Performance Max Conversions
google_pmax.conversions.description: Total conversions from Performance Max campaigns over the selected period, summed from MART_GOOGLE_CAMPAIGN_PERFORMANCE filtered to CAMPAIGN_TYPE='PERFORMANCE_MAX'.

google_pmax.cpa.title: Performance Max CPA
google_pmax.cpa.formula_human: CPA = Performance Max spend ÷ Performance Max conversions
google_pmax.cpa.description: Cost per acquisition for Performance Max: total PMax spend divided by total PMax conversions over the selected period. Lower is better.

google_pmax.roas.title: Performance Max ROAS
google_pmax.roas.formula_human: ROAS = Performance Max conversion value ÷ Performance Max spend
google_pmax.roas.description: Return on ad spend for Performance Max: total conversion value divided by total spend over the selected period, from MART_GOOGLE_CAMPAIGN_PERFORMANCE filtered to CAMPAIGN_TYPE='PERFORMANCE_MAX'.

google_pmax.spend.title: Performance Max Spend
google_pmax.spend.description: Total Performance Max spend over the selected period, summed from MART_GOOGLE_CAMPAIGN_PERFORMANCE (already in dollars) filtered to CAMPAIGN_TYPE='PERFORMANCE_MAX'.

google_search.clicks.title: Search Clicks
google_search.clicks.description: Total clicks on Google Search ads in the selected date range, summed from MART_GOOGLE_CAMPAIGN_PERFORMANCE filtered to CAMPAIGN_TYPE='SEARCH'. Each click is a user engaging with a Search ad.

google_search.conversions.title: Search Conversions
google_search.conversions.description: Total conversions from Google Search campaigns in the selected date range, summed from MART_GOOGLE_CAMPAIGN_PERFORMANCE filtered to CAMPAIGN_TYPE='SEARCH'.

google_search.quality_score.title: Search Quality Score
google_search.quality_score.formula_human: Quality Score = AVG(QUALITY_SCORE) across scored Search keywords (QUALITY_SCORE IS NOT NULL)
google_search.quality_score.description: Average Quality Score (1-10) across scored Search keywords from MART_GOOGLE_KEYWORD_PERFORMANCE (CAMPAIGN_TYPE='SEARCH', QUALITY_SCORE NOT NULL); the card also surfaces the share of keywords scoring ≥7. Higher scores lower CPCs and lift positions.

google_search.roas.title: Search ROAS
google_search.roas.formula_human: Search ROAS = SUM(CONVERSIONS_VALUE) ÷ SUM(SPEND) WHERE CAMPAIGN_TYPE='SEARCH'
google_search.roas.description: Return on ad spend for Google Search campaigns: total conversion value divided by total spend over the selected period, from MART_GOOGLE_CAMPAIGN_PERFORMANCE filtered to CAMPAIGN_TYPE='SEARCH'.

google_search.spend.title: Search Spend
google_search.spend.description: Total spend on Google Search campaigns over the selected period, summed from MART_GOOGLE_CAMPAIGN_PERFORMANCE (already in dollars) filtered to CAMPAIGN_TYPE='SEARCH'.

google_shopping.active_campaigns.title: Shopping Campaigns with Spend
google_shopping.active_campaigns.formula_human: Campaigns with Spend = COUNT(DISTINCT CAMPAIGN_ID) WHERE SPEND>0 (vs total distinct Shopping campaigns)
google_shopping.active_campaigns.description: Count of distinct Shopping campaigns (CAMPAIGN_TYPE='SHOPPING') that recorded any spend in the selected date range, from MART_GOOGLE_CAMPAIGN_PERFORMANCE; the comparison value is the total number of Shopping campaigns in the period.

google_shopping.conversion_value.title: Shopping Revenue (Conversion Value)
google_shopping.conversion_value.description: Total conversion value (revenue) attributed to Shopping campaigns over the selected period, summed from MART_GOOGLE_CAMPAIGN_PERFORMANCE filtered to CAMPAIGN_TYPE='SHOPPING'. Exposed as the 'revenue' metric and used as the numerator for Shopping ROAS.

google_shopping.roas.title: Shopping ROAS
google_shopping.roas.formula_human: Shopping ROAS = SUM(CONVERSIONS_VALUE) ÷ SUM(SPEND) WHERE CAMPAIGN_TYPE='SHOPPING'
google_shopping.roas.description: Return on ad spend for Shopping campaigns: total conversion value divided by total spend over the selected period, from MART_GOOGLE_CAMPAIGN_PERFORMANCE filtered to CAMPAIGN_TYPE='SHOPPING'.

google_shopping.spend.title: Shopping Spend
google_shopping.spend.description: Total spend on Shopping campaigns over the selected period, summed from MART_GOOGLE_CAMPAIGN_PERFORMANCE (already in dollars) filtered to CAMPAIGN_TYPE='SHOPPING'.

google_youtube.conversion_value.title: YouTube Conversion Value
google_youtube.conversion_value.description: Total conversion value (revenue) attributed to YouTube/Video campaigns over the selected period, summed from MART_GOOGLE_YOUTUBE_DAILY (VIDEO campaigns only). This is the numerator used for video ROAS.

google_youtube.conversions.title: YouTube Conversions
google_youtube.conversions.description: Total conversions from YouTube/Video campaigns over the selected period, summed from MART_GOOGLE_YOUTUBE_DAILY (VIDEO campaigns only).

google_youtube.cpv.title: YouTube Cost Per View
google_youtube.cpv.description: Average cost paid for each video view across YouTube campaigns over the selected period, from MART_GOOGLE_YOUTUBE_DAILY. Lower CPV means more efficient reach for the budget.

google_youtube.impressions.title: YouTube Impressions
google_youtube.impressions.description: Total ad impressions across all YouTube/Video campaigns over the selected period, summed from MART_GOOGLE_YOUTUBE_DAILY. An impression counts each time an ad is shown, whether or not it becomes a view.

google_youtube.roas.title: YouTube (Video) ROAS
google_youtube.roas.description: Return on ad spend for YouTube/Video campaigns: total conversion value divided by total spend over the selected period, from MART_GOOGLE_YOUTUBE_DAILY.

google_youtube.spend.title: YouTube Spend
google_youtube.spend.description: Total spend across all YouTube/Video campaigns over the selected period, summed from MART_GOOGLE_YOUTUBE_DAILY (already in dollars).

google_youtube.video_views.title: YouTube Video Views
google_youtube.video_views.description: Total video views across all YouTube/Video campaigns over the selected period, summed from MART_GOOGLE_YOUTUBE_DAILY. A view counts when a user watches enough of the video ad to register as a view.

google_youtube.view_rate.title: YouTube View Rate
google_youtube.view_rate.description: Percentage of video ad impressions that resulted in a view, from MART_GOOGLE_YOUTUBE_DAILY. Higher view rates indicate the creative is engaging enough to hold viewers past the skip threshold.

email.active_flows.title: Active Flows
email.active_flows.formula_human: Active Flows = COUNT(DISTINCT FLOW_ID) WHERE FLOW_STATUS = 'live' over the period
email.active_flows.description: Number of distinct automation flows in live status that sent during the period. Counts only live flows, not paused or draft automations.

email.bounce_rate.title: Email Bounce Rate
email.bounce_rate.description: Percentage of sent emails that could not be delivered and bounced. A rising bounce rate erodes sender reputation and signals stale or low-quality list data.

email.click_rate.title: Email Click Rate
email.click_rate.formula_human: Click Rate = SUM(TOTAL_UNIQUE_CLICKS) / SUM(TOTAL_SENDS) * 100 (on klaviyo-email); on klaviyo-flows it is AVG(per-flow CLICK_RATE)
email.click_rate.description: Percentage of delivered emails that received a click. Primary definition (klaviyo-email) is pooled unique clicks over sends; the klaviyo-flows view averages each flow-day's precomputed click rate.

email.ctor.title: Email Click-to-Open Rate (CTOR)
email.ctor.description: Of the people who opened an email, the percentage that also clicked. CTOR isolates content quality from subject-line and deliverability effects since it only counts engaged openers.

email.emails_sent.title: Emails Sent
email.emails_sent.formula_human: Emails Sent = SUM(TOTAL_SENDS) over the period
email.emails_sent.description: Total number of marketing emails delivered to recipients over the period, summed from the email overview mart. This is the denominator behind every rate-based email KPI.

email.open_rate.title: Email Open Rate
email.open_rate.formula_human: Open Rate = SUM(TOTAL_UNIQUE_OPENS) / SUM(TOTAL_SENDS) * 100 (on klaviyo-email); on klaviyo-flows it is AVG(per-flow OPEN_RATE)
email.open_rate.description: Share of delivered emails opened at least once, computed as unique opens over total sends. Headline indicator of subject-line strength and list engagement; the klaviyo-flows view averages each flow-day's precomputed open rate instead.

email.unsubscribe_rate.title: Email Unsubscribe Rate
email.unsubscribe_rate.formula_human: Unsubscribe Rate = EMAIL_UNSUBSCRIBED (MART_KLAVIYO_LIST_HEALTH) / total customers (COUNT(*) of MART_CUSTOMER_HEALTH) * 100
email.unsubscribe_rate.source_code_ref: backend/app/features/churn_analysis/metrics/service.py:82-93 (unsubscribe-rate = unsubscribed_count / total_customers); backend/app/repositories/churn_analysis.py:275 (EMAIL_UNSUBSCRIBED from MART_KLAVIYO_LIST_HEALTH), :261 (TOTAL_CUSTOMERS = COUNT(*) MART_CUSTOMER_HEALTH)
email.unsubscribe_rate.description: Email unsubscribes from Klaviyo list health divided by total customer count, expressed as a percentage. Surfaced on the churn-analysis dashboard as an email-channel engagement-erosion signal. (Note: the klaviyo-email mart also exposes an email-internal unsubscribe_rate = TOTAL_UNSUBSCRIBES / TOTAL_SENDS, but this metric_uid is registered on churn-analysis, so the customer-base denominator is authoritative.)

sms.click_rate.title: SMS Click Rate
sms.click_rate.formula_human: SMS Click Rate = AVG(daily CLICK_RATE) from MART_SMS_PERFORMANCE (klaviyo-sms); on sms-performance = COUNT(DISTINCT Clicked SMS) / COUNT(DISTINCT Received SMS) * 100 from KLAVIYO.EVENT
sms.click_rate.description: How effectively SMS messages drive taps. klaviyo-sms averages the precomputed daily click-rate values in the SMS mart (CLICK_RATE = clicks/sends); sms-performance computes distinct clicked SMS over distinct received SMS from raw events.

sms.revenue.title: SMS Revenue
sms.revenue.formula_human: SMS Revenue = SUM(TOTAL_REVENUE) from MART_SMS_PERFORMANCE (klaviyo-sms); on sms-performance = SUM of Placed Order event value attributed to SMS-engaged recipients from KLAVIYO.EVENT
sms.revenue.description: Total revenue attributed to the SMS channel over the period. klaviyo-sms sums TOTAL_REVENUE from the SMS performance mart; sms-performance sums 'Placed Order' event values (TRY_PARSE_JSON(CUSTOM_PROPERTY_FIELDS):value) for campaign/flow-attributed SMS recipients.

sms.revenue_per_sms.title: Revenue per SMS
sms.revenue_per_sms.description: Average revenue generated per SMS sent, dividing total SMS-attributed revenue by total sends. Core efficiency metric for justifying SMS send volume against carrier costs.

sms.sms_sent.title: SMS Sent
sms.sms_sent.formula_human: SMS Sent = SUM(TOTAL_SENDS) from MART_SMS_PERFORMANCE (klaviyo-sms); on sms-performance = COUNT(DISTINCT ID) WHERE TYPE='Received SMS' from KLAVIYO.EVENT
sms.sms_sent.description: Total SMS messages sent/received over the period. klaviyo-sms sums TOTAL_SENDS from the SMS performance mart; sms-performance counts distinct 'Received SMS' events. This is the volume denominator for the SMS rate metrics.

sms.unsubscribe_rate.title: SMS Unsubscribe Rate
sms.unsubscribe_rate.formula_human: SMS Unsubscribe Rate = AVG(daily OPT_OUT_RATE) from MART_SMS_PERFORMANCE (klaviyo-sms); on sms-performance = COUNT(DISTINCT 'Unsubscribed from SMS Marketing') / COUNT(DISTINCT 'Received SMS') * 100 from KLAVIYO.EVENT
sms.unsubscribe_rate.description: SMS opt-out rate over the period. klaviyo-sms averages the precomputed daily OPT_OUT_RATE (TOTAL_OPT_OUTS / TOTAL_SENDS); sms-performance computes distinct SMS opt-out events over distinct received SMS. A rising rate signals message fatigue or poor targeting. (The generic registry hint mixing in 'email unsubscribes / total customers' is a cross-channel mismatch and is excluded — this is an SMS-channel metric.)

magento.active_customers.title: Active Customers
magento.active_customers.formula_human: Active Customers = count of distinct customers with last order within 90 days of the period end (last_order_date >= date_to - 90 days)
magento.active_customers.formula_explanation: Counts customers who have engaged with your brand in the last 90 days through any activity: purchases, email opens, site visits, or other tracked events. The active rate shows database health.
magento.active_customers.description: Customers with order activity in the last 90 days, from MART_CUSTOMER_SUMMARY. Active Rate divides this by total customers.

magento.active_customers_30d.title: Active Customers (30d)
magento.active_customers_30d.formula_human: Active Customers (30d) = count of purchasing customers whose days_since_last_order <= 30
magento.active_customers_30d.description: Customer-RFM card: purchasing customers active in the trailing 30 days (SUM(CASE WHEN days_since_last_order <= 30)), from MART_CUSTOMER_RFM joined to MART_CUSTOMER_SUMMARY.

magento.aov.title: Average Order Value
magento.aov.formula_explanation: Average Order Value divides total revenue by the count of Magento orders for the period. Magento orders (rather than GA4 purchases) are used for financial accuracy.
magento.aov.source_code_ref: backend/app/repositories/magento_store_overview.py:664-701 (_build_aov_metric, calculation 'GROSS_REVENUE / TOTAL_ORDERS'); backend/app/repositories/magento_orders_fulfillment.py:768-789; dbt mart_ecom_daily.sql:103-106 (aov = gross_revenue / total_orders)
magento.aov.description: Average Order Value for the Magento store: total gross order revenue divided by the count of Magento orders over the period. The store-native definition uses GROSS_REVENUE / TOTAL_ORDERS (not net revenue and not GA4 purchases).

magento.avg_churn_risk_score.title: Average Churn Risk Score
magento.avg_churn_risk_score.formula_human: Average Churn Risk = AVG(churn_risk_score) (0-100 scale) across customers in scope
magento.avg_churn_risk_score.description: Mean per-customer churn risk score. On churn-analysis/cohort-analysis it is AVG(CHURN_RISK_SCORE) from MART_CUSTOMER_HEALTH; on customer-rfm AVG(CHURN_RISK_SCORE) from MART_CUSTOMER_RFM. Scope (acquisition cohort vs existence) varies by dashboard but the formula is the same mean.

magento.avg_customer_lifespan_days.title: Avg Days Between Orders
magento.avg_customer_lifespan_days.formula_human: Avg Customer Lifespan = AVG(DATEDIFF(day, first_order_date, last_order_date)) across customers with >=1 order
magento.avg_customer_lifespan_days.formula_explanation: This card reports average customer lifespan in days - the span between a customer's first and last order, averaged across the base. Despite the label, the backend computes lifespan (first-to-last order gap), not the gap between consecutive orders. Lower values indicate customers churn sooner; longer spans indicate sustained relationships.
magento.avg_customer_lifespan_days.description: Average span in days between a customer's first and last order (despite the 'Avg Days Between Orders' label, backend computes lifespan, not inter-order gap). From MART_CUSTOMER_SUMMARY.

magento.avg_days_between_orders.title: Avg Days Between Orders
magento.avg_days_between_orders.formula_human: Avg Days Between Orders = average of per-row AVG_DAYS_BETWEEN_ORDERS over rows with a positive gap
magento.avg_days_between_orders.formula_explanation: Average gap in days between a customer's orders, computed as the mean of daily AVG_DAYS_BETWEEN_ORDERS values over rows with data. Shorter gaps indicate more frequent purchasing.
magento.avg_days_between_orders.source_code_ref: backend/app/repositories/magento_customer_analytics.py:198-210 (_build_avg_days_between_orders_metric, 'AVG(AVG_DAYS_BETWEEN_ORDERS)'), :361-370 (weighted avg over rows where value>0); dbt mart_customer_segments_daily.sql:86 (avg(case when days_since_prev_order > 0 then days_since_prev_order end))
magento.avg_days_between_orders.description: Average gap in days between a customer's consecutive orders. Backend averages the daily per-segment AVG_DAYS_BETWEEN_ORDERS column across rows that have a positive value; the underlying mart column is avg(days_since_prev_order) where days_since_prev_order > 0. Shorter gaps indicate more frequent purchasing.

magento.avg_health_score.title: Average Health Score
magento.avg_health_score.formula_human: Average Health Score = AVG(health_score) (0-100) across scored customers; health_score = 100 - churn_probability
magento.avg_health_score.description: Mean per-customer health score from MART_CUSTOMER_HEALTH. Health score is the inverse of churn probability (100 - churn).

magento.avg_items_per_order.title: Avg Items per Order
magento.avg_items_per_order.formula_human: Avg Items per Order = SUM(TOTAL_ITEMS_ORDERED) / SUM(TOTAL_ORDERS) over the period
magento.avg_items_per_order.formula_explanation: Average number of items per order, computed by dividing total items ordered by total orders over the period from MART_PRODUCT_AFFINITY_DAILY. Higher values indicate more successful cross-selling and basket building.
magento.avg_items_per_order.description: Average items per order from MART_PRODUCT_AFFINITY_DAILY. Higher values indicate better basket building / cross-sell.

magento.avg_orders_per_customer.title: Avg Orders per Customer
magento.avg_orders_per_customer.formula_human: Avg Orders per Customer = total orders / unique customers (cohort: AVG(TOTAL_ORDERS) per customer; magento-customer-analytics: SUM(TOTAL_ORDERS)/SUM(UNIQUE_CUSTOMERS))
magento.avg_orders_per_customer.formula_explanation: The average number of orders placed per purchasing customer. It measures purchase repetition - how many times a typical customer buys before they stop.
magento.avg_orders_per_customer.description: Average purchase frequency per customer. On cohort/retention it is AVG(TOTAL_ORDERS) over MART_CUSTOMER_SUMMARY; on magento-customer-analytics it is TOTAL_ORDERS / UNIQUE_CUSTOMERS from MART_CUSTOMER_SEGMENTS_DAILY.

magento.avg_rating.title: Average Rating
magento.avg_rating.formula_human: Average Rating = AVG(NULLIF(AVG_RATING,0)) star rating (1-5) over the period; 7-day card averages daily means
magento.avg_rating.formula_explanation: Computed from the 7-day trend ending at date_to: the backend takes each day's average star rating and then averages those daily values (an unweighted mean of daily means). The comparison value is the same calculation over the previous period's 7-day window.
magento.avg_rating.description: Mean product-review star rating from MART_CUSTOMER_FEEDBACK_DETAIL (rated reviews only). The sparkline takes each day's average rating; the headline is the mean over the window.

magento.bundle_penetration.title: Bundle Penetration
magento.bundle_penetration.description: Percentage of orders containing a bundle product, from MART_PRODUCT_AFFINITY_DAILY.

magento.cancellation_rate.title: Cancellation Rate
magento.cancellation_rate.formula_human: Cancellation Rate = canceled orders / total orders * 100
magento.cancellation_rate.formula_explanation: Share of orders that were canceled over the period. A rising rate points to stock-outs, payment failures, or customer-side cancellations.
magento.cancellation_rate.description: Share of orders that were canceled over the period. Backend computes CANCELED_ORDERS / TOTAL_ORDERS * 100 from the fulfillment mart, where canceled_orders = count(distinct order_id where order_status='canceled'). A rising rate points to stock-outs, payment failures, or customer-side cancellations.

magento.cart_abandonment_rate.title: Cart Abandonment Rate
magento.cart_abandonment_rate.formula_human: Cart Abandonment Rate = abandoned carts / (abandoned carts + converted carts) * 100
magento.cart_abandonment_rate.formula_explanation: Share of carts that were started but never converted to an order, computed as abandoned carts over all carts (abandoned plus converted). It measures checkout friction.
magento.cart_abandonment_rate.description: Share of carts started but never converted to an order, computed as abandoned carts over all carts (abandoned plus converted). Backend re-derives the rate from summed cart counts in the fulfillment mart; cart counts come from fct_cart_abandonment by quote_id and cart_status. It measures checkout friction.

magento.clv.title: Average CLV
magento.clv.formula_human: Average CLV = GROSS_REVENUE / UNIQUE_CUSTOMERS (magento-customer-analytics); equivalently AVG(NET_LTV) / AVG(ACTUAL_LTV) per customer on the customer-LTV/RFM/retention/cohort dashboards
magento.clv.formula_explanation: Average Customer Lifetime Value is the mean net lifetime value across all customers who have generated positive value. It reflects the typical total worth of a retained customer to the business.
magento.clv.description: Average lifetime value per customer. The registry-cited path (magento-customer-analytics) computes GROSS_REVENUE/UNIQUE_CUSTOMERS from MART_CUSTOMER_SEGMENTS_DAILY; the customer-LTV/RFM/retention/cohort dashboards compute it as the per-customer mean of NET_LTV (MART_CUSTOMER_SUMMARY) or ACTUAL_LTV (MART_CUSTOMER_LTV). Distinct from total_clv (sum), median_clv (median), predicted_clv (sum placeholder).

magento.clv_at_risk.title: CLV at Risk
magento.clv_at_risk.formula_human: CLV at Risk = SUM(NET_LTV) for customers with CHURN_RISK_LEVEL IN ('High','Critical')
magento.clv_at_risk.formula_explanation: Total customer lifetime value from customers at critical churn risk. This represents the revenue exposure if these customers churn without intervention.
magento.clv_at_risk.description: Net lifetime value tied up in High/Critical churn-risk customers (revenue exposure if they churn). Joins MART_CUSTOMER_HEALTH (risk level) to MART_CUSTOMER_SUMMARY (NET_LTV), scoped to the acquisition cohort. Distinct from clv_at_risk_critical (Critical-only on customer-health).

magento.clv_at_risk_critical.title: CLV at Risk (Critical)
magento.clv_at_risk_critical.formula_human: CLV at Risk (Critical) = SUM(NET_LTV) for customers with CHURN_RISK_LEVEL = 'Critical' (churn score >= 80)
magento.clv_at_risk_critical.description: Customer-health card: net lifetime value of customers at Critical churn-risk level only. Distinct from clv_at_risk (High+Critical on customer-ltv/churn-analysis).

magento.completed_orders.title: Completed Orders
magento.completed_orders.formula_human: Completed Orders = count of distinct orders with order_status = 'complete'
magento.completed_orders.formula_explanation: Number of orders that reached the completed state over the period. It is the success endpoint of the fulfillment funnel.
magento.completed_orders.source_code_ref: backend/app/repositories/magento_orders_fulfillment.py:698-717 (_build_completed_orders_metric, 'SUM(COMPLETED_ORDERS)'); backend/app/repositories/magento_store_overview.py:141 (SUM(COMPLETED_ORDERS)); dbt mart_ecom_daily.sql:73 / mart_orders_fulfillment_daily.sql:45 (count distinct order_id where order_status='complete')
magento.completed_orders.description: Number of orders that reached the completed status over the period - the success endpoint of the fulfillment funnel. Raw count: count(distinct order_id) filtered to order_status='complete', summed across the period. A direct subset of total orders.

magento.critical_risk_count.title: Critical Risk Customers
magento.critical_risk_count.formula_human: Critical Risk Customers = COUNT(customers WHERE CHURN_RISK_LEVEL = 'Critical') (churn score >= 80)
magento.critical_risk_count.formula_explanation: Counts customers with 80% or higher predicted churn probability. These customers are most likely to stop purchasing without intervention.
magento.critical_risk_count.description: Count of customers at Critical churn-risk level (>=80% predicted churn) from MART_CUSTOMER_HEALTH.

magento.customers_with_clv.title: Customers with CLV
magento.customers_with_clv.formula_human: Customers with CLV = COUNT(ACTUAL_LTV) for acquisition-cohort customers with a non-null lifetime value
magento.customers_with_clv.formula_explanation: Counts the customers acquired in the selected period who have a computed lifetime value in MART_CUSTOMER_LTV. This is the denominator behind the average and median CLV metrics, indicating how many customers the value figures are based on.
magento.customers_with_clv.description: Count of acquisition-cohort customers that have a computed lifetime value in MART_CUSTOMER_LTV. This is the denominator behind avg/median CLV.

magento.customers_with_health_scores.title: Customers with Scores
magento.customers_with_health_scores.formula_human: Customers with Scores = COUNT(customers WHERE health_score IS NOT NULL)
magento.customers_with_health_scores.description: Count of customers with a computed health score (usable churn prediction) from MART_CUSTOMER_HEALTH. This is the scored population behind every other health metric.

magento.first_purchase_aov.title: First Purchase AOV
magento.first_purchase_aov.formula_human: First Purchase AOV = SUM(FIRST_ORDER_REVENUE) / total new customers acquired
magento.first_purchase_aov.formula_explanation: First Purchase Average Order Value divides total first-order revenue by the number of newly acquired customers in the period. It captures how much a customer typically spends on their very first purchase.
magento.first_purchase_aov.description: Average value of a customer's first order: total first-order revenue (MART_CUSTOMER_ACQUISITION) divided by new customers acquired (MART_CUSTOMER_SUMMARY first-order count).

magento.gross_revenue.title: Gross Revenue
magento.gross_revenue.formula_human: Gross Revenue = sum of order grand_total over the period
magento.gross_revenue.formula_explanation: Total gross store revenue over the selected period, summed from the Magento daily mart. It is the top-line measure of store performance.
magento.gross_revenue.description: Total gross store revenue over the selected period, summed from the Magento daily mart (SUM(GROSS_REVENUE)), which itself is sum(grand_total) per order. It is the top-line measure of store performance and the base for AOV.

magento.guest_checkout_rate.title: Guest Checkout Rate
magento.guest_checkout_rate.formula_human: Guest Checkout Rate = orders where customer_type='guest' / total orders * 100
magento.guest_checkout_rate.formula_explanation: Share of orders placed without a registered account, computed from MART_MAGENTO_DAILY. A high rate represents an opportunity to convert guests into known, retainable customers.
magento.guest_checkout_rate.source_code_ref: backend/app/repositories/magento_customer_analytics.py:212-224 (_build_guest_checkout_rate_metric), :528-575 (_fetch_guest_checkout_rate, 'SUM(CASE WHEN customer_type=guest THEN total_orders) / SUM(total_orders) * 100'); dbt mart_ecom_daily.sql:55-59 (customer_type classification), :72 (total_orders)
magento.guest_checkout_rate.description: Share of orders placed without a registered account. Backend computes SUM(orders where customer_type='guest') / SUM(total_orders) * 100 directly against MART_MAGENTO_DAILY. A high rate represents an opportunity to convert guests into known, retainable customers.

magento.high_risk_customers.title: High-Risk Customers
magento.high_risk_customers.formula_human: High-Risk Customers = COUNT(customers WHERE CHURN_RISK_LEVEL IN ('High','Critical')) (churn score >= 60)
magento.high_risk_customers.formula_explanation: Counts customers whose mart-assigned churn-risk level is High or Critical (churn score of 60 or above). These are the customers most likely to lapse without intervention.
magento.high_risk_customers.source_code_ref: backend/app/repositories/churn_analysis.py:266 (COUNT(CASE WHEN churn_risk_level IN High/Critical) as HIGH_RISK_CUSTOMERS)
magento.high_risk_customers.description: Count of customers at High or Critical churn-risk level from MART_CUSTOMER_HEALTH (churn-analysis dashboard). Percentage divides by customers with a prediction.

magento.journey_conversion_rate.title: Conversion Rate
magento.journey_conversion_rate.formula_human: Conversion Rate = SUM(CASE WHEN IS_CONVERSION THEN 1 END) / COUNT(*) touchpoints * 100
magento.journey_conversion_rate.description: Share of journey touchpoints in MART_CUSTOMER_JOURNEY that are conversions (IS_CONVERSION=TRUE), per period. Conversions correspond to orders.

magento.journey_engagement_rate.title: Engagement Rate
magento.journey_engagement_rate.formula_human: Engagement Rate = SUM(CASE WHEN TOUCHPOINT_VALUE > 0 THEN 1 END) / COUNT(*) touchpoints * 100 (proxy)
magento.journey_engagement_rate.description: Proxy engagement: share of MART_CUSTOMER_JOURNEY touchpoints carrying a non-zero value. The mart has no native engagement field, so non-zero touchpoint value stands in for engagement.
