magento.journey_sessions.title: Total Sessions
magento.journey_sessions.formula_human: Total Sessions = COUNT(*) journey touchpoint rows in the period
magento.journey_sessions.description: Count of every touchpoint row in MART_CUSTOMER_JOURNEY within the period; each touchpoint stands in for a session-level interaction.

magento.journey_users.title: Total Users
magento.journey_users.formula_human: Total Users = COUNT(DISTINCT CUSTOMER_ID) appearing in journey data during the period
magento.journey_users.source_code_ref: backend/app/repositories/customer_journey.py:436 (COUNT(DISTINCT CUSTOMER_ID) as CURRENT_VALUE for users KPI)
magento.journey_users.description: Distinct customers appearing in MART_CUSTOMER_JOURNEY within the period (touchpoints deduplicated to unique people).

magento.median_clv.title: Median CLV
magento.median_clv.formula_human: Median CLV = MEDIAN(ACTUAL_LTV) across acquisition-cohort customers with non-null lifetime value
magento.median_clv.description: Median per-customer realized lifetime value from MART_CUSTOMER_LTV. Distinct from clv (mean) and total_clv (sum); robust to whale skew.

magento.net_revenue.title: Net Revenue
magento.net_revenue.formula_human: Net Revenue = SUM(NET_REVENUE) from Magento orders over the period (revenue source of truth)
magento.net_revenue.formula_explanation: Total revenue generated from purchases over the selected period, summed from the daily funnel mart. The trend compares against the prior equivalent period.
magento.net_revenue.description: Sum of NET_REVENUE from MART_CEO_DAILY_PULSE (Magento source of truth) on ceo-pulse/monthly-review/quarterly-review/cro/funnel/website-performance. On customer-journey, revenue is SUM(TOUCHPOINT_VALUE WHERE IS_CONVERSION) from MART_CUSTOMER_JOURNEY. This is a raw summed column, not a ratio.

magento.new_customer_order_share.title: New Customer Revenue %
magento.new_customer_order_share.formula_human: New Customer Revenue % = SUM(NEW_CUSTOMER_ORDERS) / SUM(TOTAL_ORDERS) * 100 (order-share proxy)
magento.new_customer_order_share.formula_explanation: Share of the period's orders that came from new customers, used as a proxy for new-customer revenue mix. Computed in SQL against MART_CEO_DAILY_PULSE as new customer orders over total orders. Note: this is order-share based, not strictly dollar-revenue based.
magento.new_customer_order_share.description: Monthly-review card: share of orders from new customers, used as a proxy for new-customer revenue mix. Computed from MART_CEO_DAILY_PULSE (order-share based, not dollar-revenue).

magento.new_customers.title: New Customers
magento.new_customers.formula_human: New Customers = count of distinct customers whose first_order_date falls within the period (quarterly-review uses SUM(NEW_CUSTOMER_ORDERS))
magento.new_customers.formula_explanation: Counts customers whose profile was created within the last 30 days. This represents fresh acquisition activity and indicates the effectiveness of marketing campaigns at driving new customer growth.
magento.new_customers.description: First-time customers acquired in the period. On customer-acquisition/customer-overview/magento-customer-analytics it is a distinct first-order-date count from MART_CUSTOMER_SUMMARY / MART_CUSTOMER_ACQUISITION.NEW_CUSTOMERS; on quarterly-review it is SUM(NEW_CUSTOMER_ORDERS) from MART_CEO_DAILY_PULSE. Raw count.

magento.nps_score.title: NPS Score
magento.nps_score.formula_human: NPS Score = AVG(AVG_RATING) star rating (1-5) over the period, used as a satisfaction proxy (not a true -100..+100 NPS)
magento.nps_score.formula_explanation: Magento reviews carry no true NPS survey, so this metric repurposes the average star rating (1-5 scale) from MART_CUSTOMER_FEEDBACK_DETAIL as an NPS proxy. Despite the -100 to +100 label, the value is the mean star rating for the period, and the change is the simple difference versus the prior period.
magento.nps_score.description: Magento reviews carry no true NPS survey, so this repurposes the average star rating from MART_CUSTOMER_FEEDBACK_DETAIL as an NPS proxy. Despite the -100..+100 label the value is the mean star rating; same underlying field as avg_rating.

magento.orders.title: Total Orders
magento.orders.formula_human: Total Orders = count of distinct orders placed over the period
magento.orders.formula_explanation: Total number of orders placed over the period, summed from the Magento daily mart. It is the core volume metric for the store.
magento.orders.description: Total number of orders placed over the period, summed from the Magento daily mart (SUM(TOTAL_ORDERS)), where total_orders = count(distinct order_id) per day. It is the core volume metric for the store and the denominator for AOV, cancellation/refund/completion rates.

magento.predicted_clv.title: Predicted CLV
magento.predicted_clv.formula_human: Predicted CLV = SUM(ACTUAL_LTV) across the acquisition cohort (placeholder; no forward-looking prediction in the mart, so equals Total CLV)
magento.predicted_clv.formula_explanation: Intended to show total predicted future lifetime value, but the mart does not yet carry a forward-looking prediction, so the backend returns the sum of ACTUAL_LTV (the realized total) as a placeholder. The value therefore matches Total CLV until a prediction model is wired in.
magento.predicted_clv.description: Intended as total predicted future LTV, but the mart has no forward-looking prediction, so the backend returns SUM(ACTUAL_LTV) (realized total) as a placeholder. Equals total_clv until a model is wired in; distinct node because semantics/label differ.

magento.processing_orders.title: Processing Orders
magento.processing_orders.formula_human: Processing Orders = max(0, total orders - completed orders - canceled orders); at runtime the order-totals source lacks a canceled count so it reduces to max(0, total orders - completed orders)
magento.processing_orders.formula_explanation: Orders that are neither completed nor canceled - i.e. still in-flight. At runtime the order-totals source lacks a canceled count, so the value effectively reduces to TOTAL_ORDERS minus COMPLETED_ORDERS (clamped at zero).
magento.processing_orders.source_code_ref: backend/app/repositories/magento_orders_fulfillment.py:719-737 (_build_processing_orders_metric, 'TOTAL_ORDERS - COMPLETED_ORDERS - CANCELED_ORDERS'), :599-616 (_calculate_order_totals omits CANCELED_ORDERS so .get default 0); dbt mart_ecom_daily.sql:72-73 (total_orders, completed_orders)
magento.processing_orders.description: Orders that are neither completed nor canceled - still in-flight. Intended formula is TOTAL_ORDERS - COMPLETED_ORDERS - CANCELED_ORDERS, but the order-totals dict passed to this builder (from MART_MAGENTO_DAILY via _calculate_order_totals) has no CANCELED_ORDERS key, so the canceled term resolves to 0 and the effective value is max(0, total - completed).

magento.promoter_percent.title: Promoters %
magento.promoter_percent.formula_human: Promoters % = COUNT(reviews WHERE AVG_RATING >= 4) / COUNT(rated reviews) * 100
magento.promoter_percent.formula_explanation: The share of rated reviews that are promoters, where promoters are defined as 4-5 star reviews (proxy for NPS promoters). Computed in MART_CUSTOMER_FEEDBACK_DETAIL over the selected period, with the change as the simple percentage-point difference versus the prior period.
magento.promoter_percent.description: Share of rated reviews that are 4-5 stars (NPS-promoter proxy), from MART_CUSTOMER_FEEDBACK_DETAIL over the period.

magento.refund_rate.title: Refund Rate
magento.refund_rate.formula_human: Refund Rate = refunded orders / total orders * 100
magento.refund_rate.formula_explanation: Share of orders that were refunded over the period. A rising rate signals product, quality, or expectation problems.
magento.refund_rate.description: Share of orders that were refunded over the period. Backend computes REFUNDED_ORDERS / TOTAL_ORDERS * 100 from the fulfillment mart, where refunded_orders = count(distinct order_id) from fct_ecom_refund. A rising rate signals product, quality, or expectation problems.

magento.repeat_rate.title: Repeat Purchase Rate
magento.repeat_rate.formula_human: Repeat Purchase Rate = repeat customers / unique customers * 100 (repeat customer = TOTAL_ORDERS >= 2; or SUM(REPEAT_CUSTOMERS)/SUM(UNIQUE_CUSTOMERS) on segment/affinity marts)
magento.repeat_rate.formula_explanation: Share of customers who placed more than one order, dividing repeat customers by unique customers. It measures loyalty and retention strength.
magento.repeat_rate.description: Percentage of customers who placed 2+ orders. On retention/cohort/rfm it counts MART_CUSTOMER_SUMMARY customers with TOTAL_ORDERS>=2 over all purchasers; on magento-customer-analytics and product-affinity it is SUM(REPEAT_CUSTOMERS)/SUM(UNIQUE_CUSTOMERS)*100.

magento.returning_customers.title: Returning Customers
magento.returning_customers.formula_human: Returning Customers = count of distinct customers whose first_order_date < period start AND last_order_date >= period start
magento.returning_customers.formula_explanation: Counts customers who have made two or more purchases historically. The retention rate shows what percentage of customers with at least one order have returned to purchase again.
magento.returning_customers.source_code_ref: backend/app/repositories/customer_overview.py:446-450 (RETURNING_CUSTOMERS = COUNT DISTINCT first_order_date < date_from AND last_order_date >= date_from)
magento.returning_customers.description: Customer-overview card: customers acquired before the period who placed an order within it (first_order_date < date_from AND last_order_date >= date_from), from MART_CUSTOMER_SUMMARY. Retention Rate divides by total customers.

magento.survey_responses.title: Survey Responses
magento.survey_responses.formula_human: Survey Responses = COUNT(*) rated reviews (AVG_RATING > 0) submitted in the period
magento.survey_responses.description: Volume of customer reviews with a valid rating in the period, from MART_CUSTOMER_FEEDBACK_DETAIL (the total_responses concept; this is the denominator behind promoter_percent). Raw count.

magento.top_affinity_lift.title: Top Affinity Lift
magento.top_affinity_lift.formula_human: Top Affinity Lift = MAX(LIFT) among product pairs with CO_PURCHASE_COUNT >= 5; lift = P(A and B) / (P(A) * P(B))
magento.top_affinity_lift.formula_explanation: Highest affinity lift score among product pairs from the pre-computed MART_PRODUCT_AFFINITY mart, restricted to pairs with at least 5 co-purchases. Lift above 1 indicates a positive association; the maximum surfaces the single strongest product relationship.
magento.top_affinity_lift.description: Highest affinity lift score among product pairs in MART_PRODUCT_AFFINITY, restricted to pairs with at least 5 co-purchases. Surfaces the single strongest product relationship.

magento.total_clv.title: Total CLV
magento.total_clv.formula_human: Total CLV = SUM(NET_LTV) across customers in scope (customer-ltv: SUM(ACTUAL_LTV))
magento.total_clv.formula_explanation: Sums the realized (actual) lifetime value across every customer acquired in the selected date range, drawn from MART_CUSTOMER_LTV. The date filter is applied on each customer's first_order_date, so the value reflects the total CLV booked by that acquisition cohort.
magento.total_clv.description: Total realized portfolio lifetime value. On customer-rfm/cohort it is SUM(NET_LTV) from MART_CUSTOMER_SUMMARY; on customer-ltv it is SUM(ACTUAL_LTV) from MART_CUSTOMER_LTV. Distinct from clv (mean) and median_clv (median); the per-customer LTV values aggregate up to this sum.

magento.total_customers.title: Total Customers
magento.total_customers.formula_human: Total Customers = count of distinct customers with purchase history (customer-overview: COUNT DISTINCT customer_id active in range; customer-rfm: COUNT(*) of MART_CUSTOMER_RFM)
magento.total_customers.description: Total customer base. On customer-overview it is COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) with order activity overlapping the range (first_order_date <= date_to AND last_order_date >= date_from); on customer-rfm it is COUNT(*) of MART_CUSTOMER_RFM. Raw count; parent for new/returning/active customer subsets.

magento.unique_customers.title: Unique Customers
magento.unique_customers.formula_human: Unique Customers = count of distinct customers who ordered over the period
magento.unique_customers.description: Count of unique customers who placed orders over the period. Store Overview sums UNIQUE_CUSTOMERS from MART_MAGENTO_DAILY (count(distinct customer_id) per day). Customer Analytics computes the same value for both Active and Total Customers by summing UNIQUE_CUSTOMERS from MART_CUSTOMER_SEGMENTS_DAILY (no separate activity filter). It reflects the breadth of the active buying base.

magento.units_sold.title: Units Sold
magento.units_sold.formula_human: Units Sold = sum of order item quantities over the period
magento.units_sold.formula_explanation: Total product units sold over the period, summed from the Magento daily mart. It measures physical demand independent of price.
magento.units_sold.description: Total product units sold over the period, summed from the Magento daily mart (SUM(TOTAL_UNITS_SOLD)), where total_units_sold = sum(total_qty_ordered) per order. It measures physical demand independent of price.

meta_ads.cpm.title: Meta CPM
meta_ads.cpm.formula_explanation: Cost per 1,000 impressions measures how much you pay to show your ads a thousand times. It reflects auction competition and audience demand for the placements you target.
meta_ads.cpm.description: Meta account-level cost per 1,000 impressions across all campaigns, computed from total spend divided by total impressions times 1,000.

meta_ads.frequency.title: Meta Frequency
meta_ads.frequency.formula_explanation: Average number of times each reached user saw an ad, computed from Meta impressions divided by Meta reach. The optimal range is roughly 1.5-3.0x; above 5.0x is flagged critical and below 1.0x or above 3.0x is a warning.
meta_ads.frequency.source_code_ref: backend/app/repositories/meta_overview.py:99 (ROUND(SUM(IMPRESSIONS) / NULLIF(SUM(REACH), 0), 2) as AVG_FREQUENCY); audience_performance.py:309 (FREQUENCY = META_IMPRESSIONS / META_REACH)
meta_ads.frequency.description: Meta account-level average number of times each unique person saw an ad, computed as total impressions divided by total reach (Meta-only). Same computation in audience-performance and meta-overview dashboards.

meta_ads.reach.title: Meta Reach
meta_ads.reach.formula_explanation: Estimated unique users reached via Meta Ads in the period. This is approximate because daily reach values can overlap across days, so summing them may double-count some users.
meta_ads.reach.source_code_ref: backend/app/repositories/meta_overview.py:96 (SUM(REACH) as TOTAL_REACH); builder meta_overview.py:209 _build_reach_kpi; audience_performance.py:296 META_REACH sum where PLATFORM='Meta Ads'
meta_ads.reach.description: Meta account-level estimated unique people who saw the ads at least once over the period (approximate; daily reach can overlap across days). Raw sum of the REACH mart column.

meta_ads.roas.title: Meta ROAS
meta_ads.roas.formula_explanation: Return on Ad Spend is the revenue generated for every dollar spent, using Meta's default 7-day click plus 1-day view attribution window. A ROAS of 4.0 means $4 of attributed revenue per $1 spent.
meta_ads.roas.description: Meta account-level Return on Ad Spend (revenue per dollar spent) using Meta's 7-day click + 1-day view attribution, computed as total conversion value divided by total spend.

meta_ads.spend.title: Meta Total Spend
meta_ads.spend.formula_explanation: Total advertising spend across all Meta platforms (Facebook, Instagram, Audience Network, Messenger) for the selected period. This is the raw dollar investment before any efficiency calculations.
meta_ads.spend.description: Meta account-level total advertising spend across all Meta platforms (Facebook, Instagram, Audience Network, Messenger) for the period. Raw sum of the SPEND mart column.

meta_creative.active_creatives.title: Meta Creative Active Creatives
meta_creative.active_creatives.formula_human: Active Creatives = COUNT(DISTINCT creative_id) WHERE spend > 0 over the trailing 7 days
meta_creative.active_creatives.source_code_ref: backend/app/repositories/meta_creative.py:114-118 (COUNT(DISTINCT CREATIVE_ID) WHERE DATE_DAY >= DATEADD('day',-7,...) AND SPEND > 0); builder meta_creative.py:226 calculation 'COUNT(DISTINCT CREATIVE_ID) WHERE SPEND > 0 in last 7 days'
meta_creative.active_creatives.description: Number of unique Meta creatives that received ad spend in the last 7 days (relative to the period end), showing how much of the creative library is currently active versus dormant.

meta_creative.cpc.title: Meta Creative Avg CPC
meta_creative.cpc.source_code_ref: backend/app/repositories/meta_creative.py:90 (ROUND(SUM(SPEND) / NULLIF(SUM(LINK_CLICKS), 0), 2) as AVG_CPC); builder meta_creative.py:295 calculation 'SUM(SPEND) / SUM(LINK_CLICKS)'
meta_creative.cpc.description: Average Meta creative cost per link click - total creative spend divided by total link clicks. Lower CPC means buying engagement more cheaply.

meta_creative.ctr.title: Meta Creative Avg CTR
meta_creative.ctr.source_code_ref: backend/app/repositories/meta_creative.py:89 (ROUND(SUM(LINK_CLICKS) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(IMPRESSIONS), 0), 2) as AVG_CTR); builder meta_creative.py:272 calculation 'SUM(LINK_CLICKS) * 100 / SUM(IMPRESSIONS)'
meta_creative.ctr.description: Average Meta creative click-through rate - link clicks as a percentage of impressions. The headline measure of how engaging the creative is.

meta_creative.spend.title: Meta Creative Spend
meta_creative.spend.description: Total Meta advertising spend across all creatives over the period. Raw sum of the SPEND column in the creative-performance mart.

meta_creative.total_creatives.title: Meta Creative Total Creatives
meta_creative.total_creatives.source_code_ref: backend/app/repositories/meta_creative.py:85 (COUNT(DISTINCT CREATIVE_ID) as TOTAL_CREATIVES); builder meta_creative.py:209 calculation 'COUNT(DISTINCT CREATIVE_ID)'
meta_creative.total_creatives.description: Total number of unique Meta creative assets used in ads during the selected period - the breadth of the creative library actually in market.

meta_prospecting.cost_per_1k_reach.title: Meta Prospecting Cost per 1k Reach
meta_prospecting.cost_per_1k_reach.source_code_ref: backend/app/repositories/meta_prospecting.py:98 (ROUND(SUM(SPEND) / NULLIF(SUM(REACH), 0) * 1000, 4) as COST_PER_REACH); builder meta_prospecting.py:268,293 calculation 'SUM(SPEND) / SUM(REACH) * 1000'
meta_prospecting.cost_per_1k_reach.description: Cost to reach 1,000 unique people with Meta prospecting (top-of-funnel) ads. Core efficiency metric for prospecting campaigns; lower is better, target under $6.00.

meta_prospecting.cpm.title: Meta Prospecting Avg CPM
meta_prospecting.cpm.formula_human: Avg CPM = AVG(daily campaign CPM), where per-row CPM = spend / impressions * 1000
meta_prospecting.cpm.description: Average cost per 1,000 impressions across Meta prospecting campaigns, computed as the mean of the pre-computed per-row CPM mart column (underlying spend / impressions * 1000). Industry benchmark ~$7.50.

meta_prospecting.frequency.title: Meta Prospecting Avg Frequency
meta_prospecting.frequency.formula_human: Avg Frequency = AVG(daily campaign frequency), where per-row frequency = impressions / reach
meta_prospecting.frequency.description: Average number of times each person saw a Meta prospecting ad, computed as the mean of the pre-computed per-row FREQUENCY mart column (underlying impressions / reach). For prospecting, keep under 2.

meta_prospecting.reach.title: Meta Prospecting Reach
meta_prospecting.reach.description: Total unique people reached by Meta prospecting campaigns over the period (approximate; daily reach can overlap). Raw sum of the REACH mart column.

meta_prospecting.spend.title: Meta Prospecting Spend
meta_prospecting.spend.description: Total Meta advertising spend across all prospecting campaigns for the period - investment aimed at reaching brand-new, non-customer audiences. Raw sum of the SPEND mart column.

meta_retargeting.ctr.title: Meta Retargeting CTR
meta_retargeting.ctr.formula_human: Retargeting CTR = AVG(daily ad-set CTR), where per-row CTR = clicks / impressions * 100
meta_retargeting.ctr.source_code_ref: backend/app/repositories/meta_retargeting.py:298 (AVG(...CTR...) as AVG_CTR), :320 (AVG(CTR) as CTR); builder meta_retargeting.py:226,237 calculation 'AVG(CTR)'
meta_retargeting.ctr.description: Average click-through rate across Meta retargeting campaigns, computed as the mean of the pre-computed per-row CTR mart column (underlying clicks / impressions). Warm audiences should engage more; target 2% or higher.

meta_retargeting.frequency.title: Meta Retargeting Frequency
meta_retargeting.frequency.formula_human: Avg Frequency = AVG(daily frequency), where per-row frequency = impressions / reach
meta_retargeting.frequency.source_code_ref: backend/app/repositories/meta_retargeting.py:297 (AVG(...FREQUENCY...) as AVG_FREQUENCY), :320 (AVG(FREQUENCY) as FREQUENCY); builder meta_retargeting.py:182,193 calculation 'AVG(FREQUENCY)'
meta_retargeting.frequency.description: Average number of times each person saw a Meta retargeting ad, computed as the mean of the pre-computed per-row FREQUENCY mart column (underlying impressions / reach). Optimal range roughly 1.5-3.0x; above 5.0x is critical.

meta_retargeting.reach.title: Meta Retargeting Reach
meta_retargeting.reach.source_code_ref: backend/app/repositories/meta_retargeting.py:295 (SUM(...REACH...) as TOTAL_REACH), :319 (SUM(REACH) as REACH); builder meta_retargeting.py:200,214 calculation 'SUM(REACH)'
meta_retargeting.reach.description: Total unique users who saw Meta retargeting ads over the period (approximate; daily reach can overlap across days). Raw sum of the REACH column in the retargeting-daily mart.

meta_retargeting.retargeting_fatigue.title: Meta Retargeting Fatigue
meta_retargeting.retargeting_fatigue.formula_human: Retargeting Fatigue = (historical-period AVG(CTR) - current-period AVG(CTR)) / historical-period AVG(CTR) * 100
meta_retargeting.retargeting_fatigue.source_code_ref: backend/app/repositories/meta_retargeting.py:392-394 (CASE WHEN h.CTR > 0 THEN ((h.CTR - c.CTR) / h.CTR) * 100 ... as FATIGUE_SCORE, where CTR = AVG(CTR)); builder meta_retargeting.py:243 _ep07_build_fatigue calculation '((historical_ctr - current_ctr) / historical_ctr) * 100'
meta_retargeting.retargeting_fatigue.description: Measures CTR decline of the current period versus the comparison (historical) period for Meta retargeting. Higher percentage means engagement has dropped, indicating audience fatigue. Reference target 15%.

meta_retargeting.roas.title: Meta Retargeting ROAS
meta_retargeting.roas.source_code_ref: backend/app/repositories/meta_retargeting.py:335-336,352 (SUM(CONVERSIONS_VALUE) as TOTAL_REVENUE; TOTAL_REVENUE / TOTAL_SPEND as AVG_ROAS); builder meta_retargeting.py:155 calculation 'SUM(REVENUE) / SUM(SPEND)'
meta_retargeting.roas.description: Return on Ad Spend for Meta retargeting campaigns - revenue per dollar spent, using Meta's 7-day click + 1-day view attribution. Retargeting reaches warm audiences, so target is 5.0x or higher.

meta_retargeting.spend.title: Meta Retargeting Spend
meta_retargeting.spend.formula_human: Retargeting Spend = SUM(spend) WHERE retargeting filter
meta_retargeting.spend.source_code_ref: backend/app/repositories/meta_retargeting.py:293 (SUM(...SPEND...) as TOTAL_SPEND), :318 (SUM(SPEND) as SPEND); builder meta_retargeting.py:133 calculation 'SUM(SPEND) WHERE retargeting_filter'
meta_retargeting.spend.description: Total Meta advertising spend across retargeting campaigns over the period. Raw sum of the SPEND column in the retargeting-daily mart, scoped to retargeting ad sets.

operational.active_alert_rules.title: Active Alert Rules
operational.active_alert_rules.formula_human: Active Rules = fixed literal count of 7 enabled alert rules (hardcoded SELECT 7 AS active_rules).
operational.active_alert_rules.formula_explanation: Counts the total number of alert rules that are currently enabled and monitoring your marketing metrics. Each rule defines thresholds for a specific metric (like ROAS, CPC, or Open Rate) and triggers alerts when values fall outside acceptable ranges.
operational.active_alert_rules.description: Total number of alert rules currently enabled and monitoring. Each rule defines thresholds for a specific metric (ROAS, CPC, Open Rate) and triggers alerts when values fall outside acceptable ranges. Static value (7).

operational.avg_alert_response_time.title: Avg Alert Response Time
operational.avg_alert_response_time.formula_human: Avg Response Time = fixed literal 15.5 minutes (hardcoded 15.5 AS AVG_RESPONSE_TIME_MINUTES).
operational.avg_alert_response_time.formula_explanation: Calculates the average time between alert trigger and acknowledgment over the last 7 days. Faster response times indicate efficient alert handling. Industry best practice suggests acknowledging critical alerts within 15 minutes and warnings within 1 hour.
operational.avg_alert_response_time.description: Average time between alert trigger and acknowledgment. Static placeholder value (15.5 minutes); no live data dependency.

operational.avg_sync_lag.title: Average Sync Lag
operational.avg_sync_lag.formula_human: Average Sync Lag = average across schemas of DATEDIFF(minute, MAX(_FIVETRAN_SYNCED), CURRENT_TIMESTAMP) over GA4, Google Ads, Meta Ads, Klaviyo source tables. (Sparkline returns a fixed 30 placeholder.)
operational.avg_sync_lag.formula_explanation: Calculates the average time elapsed since the last successful sync across all monitored schemas. Lower values indicate fresher data. This metric helps identify if data is becoming stale across the board.
operational.avg_sync_lag.description: Average time elapsed since the last successful Fivetran sync across all monitored source schemas. Lower values indicate fresher data. Depends on Fivetran sync timestamps, not on metric nodes.

operational.critical_alert_count.title: Critical Alerts
operational.critical_alert_count.formula_human: Critical Alerts = (1 if today's GA4 TOTAL_REVENUE < 21301 else 0) + (1 if trailing-7-day Google Ads ROAS [conversions_value / (cost_micros/1e6)] < 4.89 else 0) + (1 if trailing-7-day Meta Ads CTR [inline_link_clicks/impressions*100] < 2.0 else 0).
operational.critical_alert_count.formula_explanation: Counts active critical alerts triggered in the last 24 hours. Critical alerts indicate metrics have breached severe thresholds requiring immediate attention, such as ROAS dropping below the P10 historical baseline or revenue falling significantly below expectations.
operational.critical_alert_count.description: Count of business metrics breaching their critical threshold: today's GA4 revenue below $21,301, 7-day Google ROAS below 4.89, or 7-day Meta CTR below 2.0%. Thresholds are P10 historical baselines.

operational.critical_anomaly_count.title: Critical Anomalies
operational.critical_anomaly_count.formula_human: Critical Anomalies = count of days in the trailing 7 days where |z-score| of daily SESSIONS or daily TOTAL_REVENUE > 2 standard deviations from the mean. Warning band = |z-score| between 1.5 and 2.
operational.critical_anomaly_count.formula_explanation: Uses statistical z-scores to detect unusual patterns in key metrics like sessions and revenue. A z-score measures how many standard deviations a value is from the mean. Values beyond 2 standard deviations occur less than 5% of the time and warrant investigation.
operational.critical_anomaly_count.source_code_ref: backend/app/features/data_quality/dashboard/repository.py:317-347 (get_critical_alerts_kpi: COUNT where ABS(sessions z) > 2 OR ABS(revenue z) > 2)
operational.critical_anomaly_count.description: Statistical anomaly count using z-scores on GA4 sessions and revenue. A z-score beyond 2 standard deviations occurs <5% of the time and warrants investigation.

operational.schema_health_pct.title: Schema Health
operational.schema_health_pct.formula_human: Schema Health % = (count of schemas synced < 360 min ago) / total schemas (4) * 100. Fresh = synced < 60 min ago; acceptable = synced 60-360 min ago. Sync lag = DATEDIFF(minute, MAX(_FIVETRAN_SYNCED), now) per schema.
operational.schema_health_pct.formula_explanation: Measures the overall health of your data pipeline by tracking how recently each schema was synced. Fivetran syncs data at regular intervals, and this metric shows what percentage of your data sources are current. A 100% score means all schemas synced within 6 hours.
operational.schema_health_pct.description: Overall data-pipeline health: percentage of source schemas (GA4, Google Ads, Meta Ads, Klaviyo) synced within 6 hours. 100% means all schemas current. Depends on Fivetran sync freshness, not metric nodes.

operational.tables_monitored.title: Tables Monitored
operational.tables_monitored.formula_human: Tables Monitored = COUNT(DISTINCT TABLE_NAME) from INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TABLES where TABLE_SCHEMA IN (GA4, GOOGLE_ADS, META_ADS, KLAVIYO). (Sparkline returns a fixed 15 placeholder.)
operational.tables_monitored.formula_explanation: Shows the total number of database tables being monitored across all four data schemas. This represents the breadth of your data infrastructure. Each table contains specific marketing data that feeds into dashboard analytics.
operational.tables_monitored.description: Total number of database tables monitored across the four source schemas — the breadth of data infrastructure feeding dashboards. Depends on the source-schema table catalog, not metric nodes.

operational.warning_alert_count.title: Warning Alerts
operational.warning_alert_count.formula_human: Warning Alerts = (1 if today's GA4 TOTAL_REVENUE in [21301, 27431]) + (1 if 7-day Google Ads ROAS in [4.89, 5.91]) + (1 if 7-day Meta Ads CTR in [2.0, 2.85]).
operational.warning_alert_count.formula_explanation: Counts active warning alerts triggered in the last 24 hours. Warning alerts indicate metrics approaching critical thresholds - for example, ROAS between P10 and P25 historical baselines. These provide early warning before situations become critical.
operational.warning_alert_count.source_code_ref: backend/app/features/alerts_config/dashboard/repository.py:30-92 (get_alert_summary: warning_revenue/warning_roas/warning_ctr BETWEEN bands summed into WARNING_ALERTS)
operational.warning_alert_count.description: Count of business metrics in their warning band (between P10 and P25 historical baselines): today's GA4 revenue $21,301-$27,431, 7-day Google ROAS 4.89-5.91, or 7-day Meta CTR 2.0%-2.85%. Early warning before situations become critical.

ml.acquisition_forecast.title: Forecast New Customers (next 4w)
ml.acquisition_forecast.formula_human: Sum of the acquisition LightGBM per-entry-category predicted new customers over the next 4 weeks.
ml.acquisition_forecast.formula_explanation: Total predicted new customers across every entry category for the next four weeks (t+1..t+4). Each per-category forecast comes from the acquisition LightGBM ensemble (Tweedie base + log-smear correction + calibration + weekly reconciliation) trained on weekly new-customer counts by entry category.
ml.acquisition_forecast.description: Total predicted new customers across every entry category for t+1..t+4. Raw ensemble output (Tweedie LightGBM + smear + calibration + weekly reconciliation); summary.total_forecast_new_customers or sum of customers.parquet score column.

ml.acquisition_forecast_avg_per_category.title: Avg / Entry Category
ml.acquisition_forecast_avg_per_category.formula_human: Total 4-week forecast new customers / number of forecastable entry categories.
ml.acquisition_forecast_avg_per_category.formula_explanation: Average predicted new customers per entry category — the total 4-week forecast divided by the number of forecastable entry categories.
ml.acquisition_forecast_avg_per_category.description: Average predicted new customers per entry category. Frontend rollup over the per-category forecast points from the acquisition forecast endpoint.

ml.acquisition_forecast_top10_concentrat.title: Top-10 Concentration
ml.acquisition_forecast_top10_concentrat.formula_human: Forecast new customers of the top-10 entry categories / total forecast new customers x 100.
ml.acquisition_forecast_top10_concentrat.formula_explanation: Share of total forecast new customers concentrated in the ten highest-forecast entry categories — a frontend rollup over the per-category forecast points.
ml.acquisition_forecast_top10_concentrat.description: Share of total forecast new customers concentrated in the ten highest-forecast entry categories. Frontend rollup over the per-category forecast points.

ml.acquisition_forecast_top_category.title: Top Entry Category
ml.acquisition_forecast_top_category.formula_human: Entry category with the maximum forecast new customers; share = that category's forecast / total forecast.
ml.acquisition_forecast_top_category.formula_explanation: The single highest-forecast entry category, with its predicted new customers and its share of the total — derived in the frontend from the per-category forecast points returned by the forecast endpoint.
ml.acquisition_forecast_top_category.description: The single highest-forecast entry category with its predicted new customers and share. Derived in the frontend from the per-category forecast points.

ml.actionable_customers.title: Actionable Customers
ml.actionable_customers.formula_human: Actionable Customers = count(customers where intervention_propensity >= optimal threshold ~0.1138)
ml.actionable_customers.formula_explanation: Customers whose predicted propensity meets the optimal threshold, making them eligible for a retention campaign.
ml.actionable_customers.description: Count of scored customers whose predicted intervention propensity meets the optimal threshold, making them eligible for a retention campaign.

ml.activation_propensity.title: Avg Activation Propensity
ml.activation_propensity.formula_human: Avg Activation Propensity = mean(activation_propensity) over all scored prospects (summary score_stats.mean)
ml.activation_propensity.formula_explanation: The average predicted first-purchase (activation) propensity across all scored prospects, read from the summary's score_stats.mean, with a fallback to the mean of the activation_propensity column.
ml.activation_propensity.description: Average predicted first-purchase (activation) propensity across all scored prospects; read from summary score_stats.mean, fallback to mean of the activation_propensity column.

ml.active_customers.title: Active Customers
ml.active_customers.formula_human: Count of customers whose predicted lifecycle stage is 'Active'.
ml.active_customers.formula_explanation: Number of customers the lifecycle model places in the 'Active' stage for the prediction window. Sourced from the model's stage counts.
ml.active_customers.description: Number of customers the lifecycle LightGBM model places in the Active stage. From summary.predicted_stage_counts['Active']; ml.lifecycle_stage is the per-customer predicted_stage_90d output column.

ml.aov_uplift_vs_historical_pct.title: Predicted Growth vs Past
ml.aov_uplift_vs_historical_pct.formula_explanation: How much higher (or lower) the model's predicted order value is versus each customer's own historical average, expressed as a percent. Shown only when historical data is available; null otherwise.
ml.aov_uplift_vs_historical_pct.description: Percent uplift of the order-value model's predicted average order value versus the historical average; null when no historical baseline exists.

ml.ascending_trajectory_customers.title: Ascending Customers
ml.ascending_trajectory_customers.formula_human: Count of customers predicted to move to a higher value trajectory (upgrade / big_upgrade).
ml.ascending_trajectory_customers.formula_explanation: Number of customers whose spend trajectory is predicted to rise. These are growth opportunities worth nurturing.
ml.ascending_trajectory_customers.description: Customers whose spend trajectory is predicted to rise. summary.improve_total; collapsed from the model's 5-class predicted_trajectory_<h> via _TRAJ_MAP (upgrade/big_upgrade -> Ascending).

ml.at_risk_customers.title: At-Risk Customers
ml.at_risk_customers.formula_human: Count of customers the lifecycle model flags as at risk of churning in the horizon.
ml.at_risk_customers.formula_explanation: Count of customers the model flags as at risk of churning in the prediction horizon. These are the priority targets for retention action.
ml.at_risk_customers.description: Customers flagged at risk of churn by the lifecycle model. From summary.at_risk_of_churn_count; backed by the per-customer at_risk_of_churn flag derived from predicted stage.

ml.attributed_revenue.title: Paid Revenue
ml.attributed_revenue.formula_human: Paid Revenue = sum of the marketing-mix model's per-channel attributed_revenue across every row of the daily_attribution parquet (Google, Meta, Email, etc.).
ml.attributed_revenue.formula_explanation: Revenue attributed to paid media by the marketing-mix model, summed across all channels (Google, Meta, Email, etc.).
ml.attributed_revenue.description: Revenue attributed to paid media by the Bayesian marketing-mix model, summed across all paid channels. Raw model output rolled up by SUM; the per-channel attributed_revenue column is the model's prediction.

ml.avg_days_until_risk.title: Avg Days Until Risk
ml.avg_days_until_risk.formula_human: Mean of days_until_risk across the Critical/High-risk products.
ml.avg_days_until_risk.formula_explanation: Average number of days until projected stock-out across the high-risk products — how much lead time remains on average.
ml.avg_days_until_risk.description: Average projected days until stock-out across high-risk products. np.mean of days_until_risk collected only for risk_level in {Critical,High}.

ml.avg_grow_probability.title: Avg Growth Probability
ml.avg_grow_probability.formula_human: Avg Growth Probability = mean(pred_prob_grow) across all scored customers (summary.mean_prob_grow)
ml.avg_grow_probability.formula_explanation: The average model-predicted probability of growing wallet share across all scored customers, read from the summary's mean_prob_grow field.
ml.avg_grow_probability.description: Average model-predicted probability of growing wallet share across all scored customers; read from summary.mean_prob_grow.

ml.avg_optimal_markdown_depth.title: Avg Optimal Markdown Depth
ml.avg_optimal_markdown_depth.formula_human: Avg Optimal Markdown Depth = mean of the model's optimal_markdown_pct across all products in the product_markdowns artifact.
ml.avg_optimal_markdown_depth.formula_explanation: Average recommended markdown percentage across all products — the typical discount depth the model suggests.
ml.avg_optimal_markdown_depth.description: Average recommended markdown percentage across all products from the markdown-optimal-timing regression model — the typical discount depth it suggests. Raw mean of the per-product model output.

ml.avg_predicted_aov.title: Expected Avg Order
ml.avg_predicted_aov.formula_explanation: The average order value the model predicts across all scored customers, taken from the summary's avg_predicted_aov field.
ml.avg_predicted_aov.description: Mean predicted next order value across all scored customers, read from the order_value summary's avg_predicted_aov field.

ml.avg_seasonality_index.title: Avg Seasonality Index
ml.avg_seasonality_index.formula_explanation: Portfolio-wide average of the monthly seasonality indices (index 1.0 = a month at the yearly average). Indicates how seasonal the catalog is overall.
ml.avg_seasonality_index.description: Portfolio-wide average of every category's 12 monthly seasonality index values (index 1.0 = a month at the yearly average).

ml.avg_stage_duration_days.title: Avg Stage Duration
ml.avg_stage_duration_days.formula_human: Average number of days customers remain in their current lifecycle stage (model summary pass-through).
ml.avg_stage_duration_days.formula_explanation: Average time, in days, customers remain in their current lifecycle stage. A direct pass-through of the model's summary metric.
ml.avg_stage_duration_days.description: Direct pass-through of the lifecycle model summary metric avg_stage_duration_days. Raw model output, no derivation.

ml.best_day_of_week.title: Best Day of Week
ml.best_day_of_week.formula_human: Mode of each customer's predicted best_day_of_week across customers.
ml.best_day_of_week.formula_explanation: The weekday when the largest share of customers are predicted to engage. Your default send day.
ml.best_day_of_week.description: Most common predicted best-response weekday. df['best_day_of_week'].mode() over the per-customer timing predictions. Aggregation of a raw model output column.

ml.brand_affinity_multi_brand_customers.title: Multi-Brand Customers
ml.brand_affinity_multi_brand_customers.formula_explanation: Customers who buy across multiple brands (Explorers and Switchers) rather than staying with one.
ml.brand_affinity_multi_brand_customers.description: Count of scored customers the brand-affinity model classifies as Explorers or Switchers (buying across multiple brands).

ml.brand_affinity_single_brand_loyalists.title: Single Brand Loyalists
ml.brand_affinity_single_brand_loyalists.formula_explanation: Customers who exclusively prefer one brand, classified as Loyalists by the model.
ml.brand_affinity_single_brand_loyalists.description: Count of scored customers the brand-affinity model classifies as Loyalists (single-brand preference).

ml.brand_affinity_switcher_pool.title: Brand Switcher Pool
ml.brand_affinity_switcher_pool.formula_explanation: Customers who frequently switch between brands — the highest-opportunity audience for brand-retention campaigns.
ml.brand_affinity_switcher_pool.description: Count of scored customers the brand-affinity model classifies as brand Switchers (highest brand-retention opportunity).

ml.brand_affinity_top_brand.title: Top Brand by Affinity
ml.brand_affinity_top_brand.formula_explanation: The brand most frequently identified as a customer's top brand across all scored customers.
ml.brand_affinity_top_brand.description: The brand most frequently identified as a customer's top brand across all scored customers (most common top_brand).

ml.break_even_discount.title: Break-Even Discount
ml.break_even_discount.formula_human: Break-Even Discount = the smallest discount_pct (scanning the revenue_impact rows in ascending discount order) at which the model's net_revenue first becomes >= 0.
ml.break_even_discount.formula_explanation: The shallowest discount depth at which incremental uplift just covers the cost of the discount. Below this, the promotion loses money.
ml.break_even_discount.source_code_ref: backend/app/features/discount_response_predictions/ml_service.py:167-171 (get_dashboard_summary: first row sorted by discount_pct where net_revenue >= 0)
ml.break_even_discount.description: The shallowest discount depth at which incremental uplift just covers the discount cost (net_revenue crosses zero), from the discount-response model's revenue_impact curve. Below it the promotion loses money.

ml.buyer_breadth_categories_forecast.title: Categories Forecast
ml.buyer_breadth_categories_forecast.formula_human: Categories Forecast = count(distinct categories with a next-4w buyer forecast) = summary.n_categories
ml.buyer_breadth_categories_forecast.formula_explanation: Counts the number of distinct product categories that have a next-4-week buyer forecast. Read from summary.json (n_categories); the fallback computes df['entity'].nunique().
ml.buyer_breadth_categories_forecast.description: Number of distinct product categories that have a next-4-week buyer forecast; read from summary.json n_categories, fallback df['entity'].nunique().

ml.buyer_breadth_forecast_avg_per_catego.title: Avg / Category
ml.buyer_breadth_forecast_avg_per_catego.formula_explanation: Total forecast buyers divided by the number of forecast categories, giving the mean predicted buyers per category. Read from summary.json (mean_forecast_per_category).
ml.buyer_breadth_forecast_avg_per_catego.description: Total forecast buyers divided by number of forecast categories; mean predicted buyers per category (summary.json mean_forecast_per_category).

ml.buyer_breadth_top10_concentration.title: Top-10 Concentration
ml.buyer_breadth_top10_concentration.formula_human: Top-10 Concentration = sum(forecast_buyers of top-10 categories) / sum(all forecast_buyers)
ml.buyer_breadth_top10_concentration.formula_explanation: Share of total forecast buyers held by the 10 highest-forecast categories. Computed client-side by forecastStats() (top10_share) after sorting categories by forecast buyers descending.
ml.buyer_breadth_top10_concentration.source_code_ref: backend/app/features/ml_category_buyer_breadth_forecast/service.py:152-166 (get_forecast points by category, sorted by forecast_buyers); concentration computed in dashboard-v2 forecastStats()
ml.buyer_breadth_top10_concentration.description: Share of total forecast buyers held by the 10 highest-forecast categories; computed client-side by forecastStats() (top10_share) after sorting categories by forecast buyers descending.

ml.buyer_breadth_top_category.title: Top Category
ml.buyer_breadth_top_category.formula_human: Top Category = argmax_category(forecast_buyers); Share = top category's forecast_buyers / total forecast_buyers
ml.buyer_breadth_top_category.formula_explanation: Identifies the single category with the highest forecast buyers and shows its share of the total. Computed client-side by forecastStats() over the forecast points (top_value, top_share).
ml.buyer_breadth_top_category.source_code_ref: backend/app/features/ml_category_buyer_breadth_forecast/service.py:152-166 (per-category forecast points); top/share computed in dashboard-v2 forecastStats()
ml.buyer_breadth_top_category.description: The category with the highest forecast buyers and its share of the total; computed client-side by forecastStats() over the forecast points (top_value, top_share).

ml.cart_recovery_high_medium_pool.title: High+Medium Tier Pool
ml.cart_recovery_high_medium_pool.formula_human: Count of abandoned carts in the high + medium recovery-score tiers combined.
ml.cart_recovery_high_medium_pool.formula_explanation: Number of abandoned carts in the high and medium recovery-score tiers combined — the pool most worth a recovery touch. Derived from the summary's tier_counts map.
ml.cart_recovery_high_medium_pool.description: The pool most worth a recovery touch. Derived from summary.tier_counts (high + medium) over the cart-recovery scored output. Serving guardrail restricts to tier in {high,medium}.

ml.cart_recovery_score.title: Avg Recovery Score
ml.cart_recovery_score.formula_human: Average predicted recovery propensity across all scored abandoned carts.
ml.cart_recovery_score.formula_explanation: The average predicted recovery propensity across all scored abandoned carts, read from the summary's score_mean field with a fallback to the mean of the cart_recovery_propensity column.
ml.cart_recovery_score.description: Mean cart_recovery_propensity. summary.score_mean (fallback customers['cart_recovery_propensity'].mean()). Raw model score average; EXPERIMENTAL/data-ceiling constrained model.

ml.carts_scored.title: Abandoned Carts
ml.carts_scored.formula_human: Count of abandoned carts (quotes) scored by the recovery model this run.
ml.carts_scored.formula_explanation: How many abandoned carts (quotes) the recovery model scored this run, read from the summary's n_scored field with a fallback to the scored-rows count.
ml.carts_scored.description: How many carts the model scored. summary.n_scored (fallback len(customers)). Raw scoring count.

ml.category_affinity_customers_scored.title: Customers Scored
ml.category_affinity_customers_scored.formula_human: Customers Scored = row count of the scored-customer affinity artifact (len(df))
ml.category_affinity_customers_scored.formula_explanation: Total customers who received a category recommendation in the latest scoring run (row count of the scored-customer artifact).
ml.category_affinity_customers_scored.description: Total customers who received a category recommendation in the latest scoring run (row count of customers.parquet).

ml.category_affinity_high_confidence_cus.title: High Confidence
ml.category_affinity_high_confidence_cus.formula_human: High Confidence = count(confidence_tier=='High'); Share = high_confidence_count / total scored
ml.category_affinity_high_confidence_cus.formula_explanation: Customers where one category clearly stands out and the model is very certain. These customers are served a single top-1 recommendation.
ml.category_affinity_high_confidence_cus.description: Customers where the model's top category is in the High confidence tier (served a single top-1 recommendation); share = count / total scored.

ml.category_affinity_top1_served_custome.title: Single Recommendation
ml.category_affinity_top1_served_custome.formula_human: Single Recommendation = count(recommendation_strategy=='top1'); Share = top1_count / total scored
ml.category_affinity_top1_served_custome.formula_explanation: Customers receiving exactly one category recommendation — the model is confident enough to commit to a single best match.
ml.category_affinity_top1_served_custome.description: Customers whose recommendation strategy serves a single top-1 category; share = count / total scored.

ml.category_affinity_top3_served_custome.title: 3-Category Basket
ml.category_affinity_top3_served_custome.formula_human: 3-Category Basket = count(recommendation_strategy=='top3'); Share = top3_count / total scored
ml.category_affinity_top3_served_custome.formula_explanation: Customers receiving three category recommendations — used when the model is less certain. Suited to broader personalisation in email and homepage carousels.
ml.category_affinity_top3_served_custome.description: Customers whose recommendation strategy serves three categories; share = count / total scored.

ml.category_buyer_breadth_forecast.title: Forecast Buyers (next 4w)
ml.category_buyer_breadth_forecast.formula_human: Forecast Buyers (next 4w) = sum(category_buyer_breadth_forecast_lgbm_score) across categories x weeks (summary.total_forecast_buyers)
ml.category_buyer_breadth_forecast.formula_explanation: Adds up the LightGBM-predicted distinct-buyer (reach) score across every category for the next four weeks (t+1..t+4). The total comes from the precomputed summary.json artifact (total_forecast_buyers); the fallback sums the model score column directly.
ml.category_buyer_breadth_forecast.description: Sum of the LightGBM-predicted distinct-buyer (reach) score across every category over the next four weeks; from summary.total_forecast_buyers, fallback sums the model score column.

ml.category_purchase_propensity_customer.title: Customers Scored
ml.category_purchase_propensity_customer.formula_human: Customers Scored = row count of the category-purchase-propensity customer_predictions parquet (falls back to dashboard_summary.total_customers_scored when the parquet is absent).
ml.category_purchase_propensity_customer.formula_explanation: Total customers with a category purchase-propensity prediction in the latest run.
ml.category_purchase_propensity_customer.description: Total customers with a category purchase-propensity prediction in the latest run — the row count of the scored-customer parquet, with the summary artifact's total_customers_scored as fallback. A leaf COUNT of model output.

ml.category_purchase_propensity_high_pro.title: High Propensity Pool
ml.category_purchase_propensity_high_pro.formula_human: High Propensity Pool = high_propensity_count from the model's dashboard_summary artifact; share = high_propensity_count / customers scored * 100.
ml.category_purchase_propensity_high_pro.formula_explanation: Count of customers with high-confidence category purchase predictions — the audience most likely to convert.
ml.category_purchase_propensity_high_pro.description: Count of customers with high-confidence category purchase predictions (the audience most likely to convert), read from the category-propensity model's dashboard_summary, with its share computed against total customers scored.

ml.category_purchase_propensity_model_au.title: Model AUC
ml.category_purchase_propensity_model_au.formula_human: Model AUC = the model_auc from the dashboard_summary artifact, falling back to model_config.test_metrics.auc.
ml.category_purchase_propensity_model_au.formula_explanation: Model-quality metric read from the artifact: area under the ROC curve for the category purchase-propensity model. Higher is better (0.5 = random, 1.0 = perfect).
ml.category_purchase_propensity_model_au.description: Offline model-quality metric read from the category-propensity model's artifacts: area under the ROC curve (0.5 = random, 1.0 = perfect). A leaf metadata value taken from the summary, with the model_config test AUC as fallback.

ml.category_purchase_propensity_top_oppo.title: Top Opportunity Category
ml.category_purchase_propensity_top_oppo.formula_human: Top Opportunity Category = the top_opportunity_category string read directly from the model's dashboard_summary artifact.
ml.category_purchase_propensity_top_oppo.formula_explanation: The product category with the strongest purchase-propensity signals across all customers, surfaced from the precomputed summary artifact.
ml.category_purchase_propensity_top_oppo.description: The product category with the strongest purchase-propensity signals across all scored customers, surfaced verbatim from the category-propensity model's precomputed dashboard_summary artifact. A leaf metadata value.

ml.category_revenue_categories_forecast.title: Categories Forecast
ml.category_revenue_categories_forecast.formula_human: Categories Forecast = count(distinct categories with a next-4w revenue forecast) = summary.n_categories
ml.category_revenue_categories_forecast.formula_explanation: Counts the number of distinct product categories that have a next-4-week revenue forecast. Read from summary.json (n_categories); the fallback computes df['entity'].nunique().
ml.category_revenue_categories_forecast.description: Number of distinct product categories that have a next-4-week revenue forecast; from summary.json n_categories, fallback df['entity'].nunique().

ml.category_revenue_forecast.title: Forecast Revenue (next 4w)
ml.category_revenue_forecast.formula_human: Forecast Revenue (next 4w) = sum(category_revenue_forecast_lgbm_score) across categories x weeks (summary.total_forecast_revenue)
ml.category_revenue_forecast.formula_explanation: Adds up the LightGBM-predicted revenue across every category for the next four weeks (t+1..t+4). Read from the precomputed summary.json (total_forecast_revenue); the fallback sums the model score column directly.
ml.category_revenue_forecast.description: Sum of the LightGBM-predicted revenue across every category over the next four weeks; from summary.total_forecast_revenue, fallback sums the model score column.

ml.category_revenue_forecast_avg_per_cat.title: Avg / Category
ml.category_revenue_forecast_avg_per_cat.formula_explanation: Total forecast revenue divided by the number of forecast categories, giving the mean predicted revenue per category. Read from summary.json (mean_forecast_per_category).
ml.category_revenue_forecast_avg_per_cat.description: Total forecast revenue divided by number of forecast categories; mean predicted revenue per category (summary.json mean_forecast_per_category).

ml.category_revenue_top10_concentration.title: Top-10 Concentration
ml.category_revenue_top10_concentration.formula_human: Top-10 Concentration = sum(forecast_revenue of top-10 categories) / sum(all forecast_revenue)
ml.category_revenue_top10_concentration.formula_explanation: Share of total forecast revenue held by the 10 highest-forecast categories. Computed client-side by forecastStats() (top10_share) after sorting categories by forecast revenue descending.
ml.category_revenue_top10_concentration.source_code_ref: backend/app/features/ml_category_revenue_forecast/service.py:153-167 (get_forecast per-category points sorted by forecast_revenue); concentration in dashboard-v2 forecastStats()
ml.category_revenue_top10_concentration.description: Share of total forecast revenue held by the 10 highest-forecast categories; computed client-side by forecastStats() (top10_share).

ml.category_revenue_top_category.title: Top Category
ml.category_revenue_top_category.formula_human: Top Category = argmax_category(forecast_revenue); Share = top category's forecast_revenue / total forecast_revenue
ml.category_revenue_top_category.formula_explanation: Identifies the single category with the highest forecast revenue and shows its share of the total. Computed client-side by forecastStats() over the forecast points (top_value, top_share).
ml.category_revenue_top_category.source_code_ref: backend/app/features/ml_category_revenue_forecast/service.py:153-167 (per-category forecast points); top/share in dashboard-v2 forecastStats()
ml.category_revenue_top_category.description: The category with the highest forecast revenue and its share of the total; computed client-side by forecastStats() (top_value, top_share).

ml.channel_affinity_avg_confidence.title: Average Confidence
ml.channel_affinity_avg_confidence.formula_human: Mean predicted channel_confidence of the preferred channel across all scored customers.
ml.channel_affinity_avg_confidence.formula_explanation: Average certainty of the channel-affinity predictions. Higher means the model distinguishes channel preferences more cleanly.
ml.channel_affinity_avg_confidence.description: Average certainty of channel-affinity predictions. df['channel_confidence'].mean(). Aggregation of the raw per-customer confidence output.

ml.channel_affinity_matched_customers.title: Channel-Matched Customers
ml.channel_affinity_matched_customers.formula_human: Count of scored customers with channel_confidence > 0.6.
ml.channel_affinity_matched_customers.formula_explanation: Number of customers the model confidently assigns to a single best channel (confidence above 0.6). These can be safely routed channel-first.
ml.channel_affinity_matched_customers.description: Customers confidently assigned to a single best channel. (df['channel_confidence'] > 0.6).sum(). Threshold count over a raw model output column.

ml.channel_affinity_multi_channel_pool.title: Multi-Channel Pool
ml.channel_affinity_multi_channel_pool.formula_human: Count of scored customers whose predicted preferred_channel == 'Multi-channel'.
ml.channel_affinity_multi_channel_pool.formula_explanation: Count of customers whose predicted preference is a multi-channel mix rather than a single channel. These reward cross-channel orchestration.
ml.channel_affinity_multi_channel_pool.description: Customers whose predicted preference is a multi-channel mix. (df['preferred_channel'] == 'Multi-channel').sum(). Category count over a raw model output column.

ml.channel_affinity_top_channel.title: Top Channel
ml.channel_affinity_top_channel.formula_human: Most common predicted preferred_channel across all scored customers.
ml.channel_affinity_top_channel.formula_explanation: The single channel most customers prefer. Your default reach channel when you have to pick one.
ml.channel_affinity_top_channel.description: The single channel most customers prefer. df['preferred_channel'].mode(). Mode of a raw model output column.

ml.channels_at_saturation.title: Channels at Saturation
ml.channels_at_saturation.formula_human: Count of channels where current_spend / optimal_spend > 0.8.
ml.channels_at_saturation.formula_explanation: Number of channels already spending above 80% of their model-estimated optimal budget. Extra dollars here yield diminishing returns.
ml.channels_at_saturation.description: Channels spending above 80% of model-estimated optimal budget. Loops saturation_summary, counts where current_spend/optimal_spend > 0.8. ml.current_spend and ml.optimal_spend are the saturation-curve model output fields.

ml.churn_probability.title: Average Churn Probability
ml.churn_probability.formula_human: Average Churn Probability = mean(churn_probability) across all scored customers (summary.avg_churn_probability)
ml.churn_probability.formula_explanation: The mean probability — across every customer the model has scored — that they churn within the selected horizon (30 / 90 / 180 days). Reported as a percentage so the whole base can be summarized in one number.
ml.churn_probability.description: Mean predicted churn probability across every scored customer within the selected horizon; from summary.json, fallback mean of churn_probability column.

ml.churn_rate.title: Churn Rate
ml.churn_rate.formula_human: Churn Rate = count(churn_probability >= at-risk threshold) / total customers scored
ml.churn_rate.formula_explanation: Share of customers whose model-estimated churn probability over the horizon clears the at-risk threshold. Threshold is set per horizon during training.
ml.churn_rate.description: Share of customers whose model-estimated churn probability over the horizon clears the at-risk threshold (per-horizon, set at training).

ml.clv_at_risk.title: Total CLV at Risk
ml.clv_at_risk.formula_human: Total CLV at Risk = sum_customers(churn_probability x predicted CLV) scaled to horizon (= sum(clv_at_risk col) x days/180); Revenue at Risk = that / total predicted revenue
ml.clv_at_risk.formula_explanation: Expected revenue we lose if the predicted churn actually happens — every customer's probability × their predicted CLV, scaled to the active horizon. This is the dollar version of the risk count.
ml.clv_at_risk.description: Expected revenue lost if predicted churn occurs: per-customer churn probability x predicted CLV, summed and scaled to the active horizon. Also surfaced as a share of total predicted revenue.

ml.conversion_rate.title: Avg Conversion Rate
ml.conversion_rate.formula_human: Avg Conversion Rate = mean(conversion_rate over all offer types) (np.mean of conversion_by_type rates)
ml.conversion_rate.formula_explanation: Average conversion rate across the offer types the model evaluated. A baseline to judge any single offer type against.
ml.conversion_rate.description: Average predicted conversion rate across the offer types the model evaluated; a baseline to judge any single offer type against.

ml.customers_scored.title: Total Customers Scored
ml.customers_scored.formula_human: Customers Scored = total_customers_scored from the model's summary artifact, fallback row count of the scored-customers table
ml.customers_scored.formula_explanation: Total customers the model produced predictions for in this run, scoped to the active horizon's training cutoff. Treat it as the denominator for every other count on the page.
ml.customers_scored.description: Count of customers the model produced predictions for this run (churn / order-value / high-value variants); the denominator for other counts on each page.

ml.declining_trajectory_customers.title: Declining Customers
ml.declining_trajectory_customers.formula_human: Count of customers predicted to move to a lower value trajectory (downgrade / big_downgrade).
ml.declining_trajectory_customers.formula_explanation: Number of customers whose spend trajectory is predicted to fall. These are the revenue-at-risk customers to defend.
ml.declining_trajectory_customers.description: Customers whose spend trajectory is predicted to fall. summary.decline_total; collapsed from the 5-class predicted_trajectory_<h> via _TRAJ_MAP (downgrade/big_downgrade -> Declining).

ml.demand_forecast.title: Forecast Units (next 4w)
ml.demand_forecast.formula_human: sum(demand_forecast_lgbm_score) over all product x week rows for next 4 weeks (summary.total_forecast_units)
ml.demand_forecast.formula_explanation: Adds up the LightGBM-predicted unit demand across every product for the next four weeks (t+1..t+4). Read from the precomputed summary.json (total_forecast_units); the fallback sums the model score column directly.
ml.demand_forecast.description: Total LightGBM-predicted unit demand across every product over the next four weeks; read from summary.json (total_forecast_units), fallback sums the model score column.

ml.demand_forecast_avg_per_product.title: Avg / Product
ml.demand_forecast_avg_per_product.formula_explanation: Total forecast units divided by the number of forecast products, giving the mean predicted units per product. Read from summary.json (mean_forecast_per_product).
ml.demand_forecast_avg_per_product.description: Total forecast units divided by the number of forecast products, giving the mean predicted units per product.

ml.demand_forecast_top_product.title: Highest-Demand Product
ml.demand_forecast_top_product.formula_human: argmax(forecast_units) product; share = top forecast_units / sum(forecast_units)
ml.demand_forecast_top_product.formula_explanation: Identifies the single product with the highest forecast units and shows its share of total demand. Computed client-side by forecastStats() over the forecast points (top_value, top_share).
ml.demand_forecast_top_product.description: The single product with the highest forecast units, plus its share of total demand; computed client-side by forecastStats() over the forecast points.

ml.discount_high_responders.title: High Responders
ml.discount_high_responders.formula_human: Count of customers in the highest discount-response tier.
ml.discount_high_responders.formula_explanation: Count of customers in the highest discount-response tier — the strongest reactors to promotional pricing.
ml.discount_high_responders.description: Strongest reactors to promotional pricing. summary.high_responders_count, backed by the response_tier bucketing of prob_discount_response. ml.discount_response_probability is the per-customer model output.

ml.discount_model_auc.title: Model AUC
ml.discount_model_auc.formula_human: AUC of the discount-response classifier on the holdout set.
ml.discount_model_auc.formula_explanation: Ranking quality of the discount-response model. Closer to 1.0 means it cleanly separates customers who respond to discounts from those who don't.
ml.discount_model_auc.description: Ranking quality of the discount-response model. summary.model_auc (fallback model_config.test_metrics.auc). Raw training artifact, no derivation.

ml.discount_responsive_customers.title: Discount-Responsive Customers
ml.discount_responsive_customers.formula_human: Count of customers predicted to respond to a discount offer.
ml.discount_responsive_customers.formula_explanation: Number of customers the model predicts will respond to a discount offer. These are the customers where promotions are most efficient.
ml.discount_responsive_customers.description: Customers where promotions are most efficient. summary.discount_responsive_count over the prob_discount_response model output. ml.discount_response_probability is the per-customer model output.

ml.entry_categories_forecast.title: Entry Categories Forecast
ml.entry_categories_forecast.formula_human: Distinct count of entry categories that carry a next-4-week new-customer forecast.
ml.entry_categories_forecast.formula_explanation: How many entry categories (the product category through which a new customer first enters) carry a next-4-week new-customer forecast. Computed as the distinct entity count over the predictions table.
ml.entry_categories_forecast.description: Number of distinct entry categories (entity) in the acquisition forecast predictions table. summary.n_entry_categories or df['entity'].nunique().

ml.expected_retained_value.title: Expected Retained Value (ERV)
ml.expected_retained_value.formula_human: Expected Retained Value = (1 - avg_churn_probability) x avg_pred_clv_180d x total_customers_scored
ml.expected_retained_value.formula_explanation: Best estimate of the revenue you keep, assuming no extra retention work. Inverts churn: probability of staying × predicted CLV × population.
ml.expected_retained_value.description: Best estimate of revenue kept with no extra retention work: probability of staying x predicted CLV x population. Computed client-side from the churn summary.

ml.first_time_buyers_scored.title: One-Time Buyers Scored
ml.first_time_buyers_scored.formula_human: summary.n_scored (count of registered first-time buyers scored by the first_repeat model)
ml.first_time_buyers_scored.formula_explanation: How many one-time (first-purchase) buyers the repeat model scored this run, read from the summary's n_scored field with a fallback to the scored-rows count.
ml.first_time_buyers_scored.description: Number of one-time (first-purchase) buyers the first-repeat model scored this run; from summary.n_scored, fallback to scored-rows count.

ml.forecast_accuracy_mape.title: Forecast Accuracy (MAPE)
ml.forecast_accuracy_mape.formula_human: Mean absolute percentage error of demand forecasts on the holdout period.
ml.forecast_accuracy_mape.formula_explanation: Model-quality metric read from the training artifact: the mean absolute percentage error of forecasts in the holdout period. Lower is better.
ml.forecast_accuracy_mape.description: Model-quality metric read from model_config.forecast_accuracy_mape. Raw training artifact, no derivation.

ml.forecasted_units_90d.title: Forecasted Units (90d)
ml.forecasted_units_90d.formula_human: Sum of each product's forecasted_units_90d across all forecasted products.
ml.forecasted_units_90d.formula_explanation: Total predicted unit demand across all forecasted products over the next 90 days.
ml.forecasted_units_90d.description: Total predicted unit demand over the next 90 days. Sums forecasted_units_90d over product_forecasts. Aggregation of the raw per-product forecast output.

ml.growth_rate.title: Growth Rate
ml.growth_rate.formula_human: Growth Rate = flagged_growth_customers / total_customers_scored x 100 (summary.flagged_pct x 100)
ml.growth_rate.formula_explanation: The share of scored customers the model flags as likely to grow their wallet share / spend. Computed as flagged growth customers divided by the total scored, expressed as a percent.
ml.growth_rate.description: Share of scored customers flagged as likely to grow their wallet share / spend.

ml.growth_vs_prior.title: Growth vs Prior Year
ml.growth_vs_prior.formula_human: (forecast quarter total / actual revenue of matching weeks 52w earlier) - 1) * 100
ml.growth_vs_prior.formula_explanation: Forecasted quarter total compared against the actual revenue of the matching weeks one year earlier (the history series shifted back 52 weeks), expressed as a percent change. Requires at least 52 weeks of history.
ml.growth_vs_prior.description: Forecasted 13-week quarter total versus the actual revenue of the same 13 calendar weeks one year earlier (history shifted back 52 weeks); requires >= 52 weeks of history.

ml.high_confidence_count.title: Reliable Predictions
ml.high_confidence_count.formula_explanation: How many predictions are high-confidence. Read from the summary's high_confidence_customer_count; if absent, computed on the fly as the count of customers whose prediction confidence-interval width is at or below the 25th percentile (the tightest, most certain quartile).
ml.high_confidence_count.description: Count of order-value predictions whose confidence-interval width falls in the narrowest (<=25th percentile) quartile; from summary, fallback computed over confidence_interval_width.

ml.high_growth_products.title: High-Growth Products (>20%)
ml.high_growth_products.formula_human: Count of products whose forecasted growth_rate_pct exceeds 20%.
ml.high_growth_products.formula_explanation: Number of products whose forecasted growth rate exceeds 20% — the fastest-rising SKUs.
ml.high_growth_products.description: Fastest-rising SKUs. Counts product_forecasts rows where growth_rate_pct > 20. Threshold count over the raw per-product growth output.

ml.high_response_pool.title: High-Response Pool
ml.high_response_pool.formula_explanation: Customers with propensity above 50% -- prime candidates for premium-tier intervention spend.
ml.high_response_pool.description: Customers with predicted intervention propensity at or above 50% - prime candidates for premium-tier intervention spend.

ml.high_risk_customers.title: High-Risk Customers
ml.high_risk_customers.formula_human: High-Risk Customers = count(risk_segment in {High, Critical}), fallback count(churn_probability >= 0.387)
ml.high_risk_customers.formula_explanation: Number of customers the model places in the top two risk tiers. These are the people most likely to churn in the selected horizon and are the priority targets for retention spend.
ml.high_risk_customers.description: Customers the model places in the top two risk tiers - the priority targets for retention spend over the horizon.

ml.high_risk_products.title: High-Risk Products
ml.high_risk_products.formula_human: Count of products the model flags at Critical or High stock-out risk.
ml.high_risk_products.formula_explanation: Total count of products flagged at Critical or High stock-out risk.
ml.high_risk_products.description: Count of product_risk rows with risk_level in {Critical, High}. Direct count over the model's per-product risk_level output; treated as the base risk leaf the other stock-out metrics derive from.

ml.high_value_propensity_pool.title: High-Propensity Pool
ml.high_value_propensity_pool.formula_human: Count of customers scored into the high propensity-to-become-high-value tier.
ml.high_value_propensity_pool.formula_explanation: Number of customers scored into the high propensity-to-become-high-value tier. These are your prime nurture targets.
ml.high_value_propensity_pool.description: Prime nurture targets. summary.high_propensity_count, backed by the propensity_tier bucketing of prob_high_value. ml.high_value_propensity is the per-customer model output.

ml.highest_yoy_growth_category.title: Highest YoY Growth Category
ml.highest_yoy_growth_category.formula_explanation: The category with the largest year-over-year revenue growth.
ml.highest_yoy_growth_category.description: The category with the largest year-over-year revenue growth %, selected over the yoy_comparison artifact.

ml.immediate_action_required.title: Immediate Action (<7d)
ml.immediate_action_required.formula_human: Count of Critical/High-risk products with projected days_until_risk < 7.
ml.immediate_action_required.formula_explanation: High-risk products projected to stock out within the next 7 days — the items needing reorder action now.
ml.immediate_action_required.description: High-risk products projected to stock out within 7 days. Within the high-risk loop, increments when days_until_risk < 7.

ml.incremental_revenue.title: Total Incremental Revenue
ml.incremental_revenue.formula_human: Sum of channel-level incremental revenue the Bayesian MMM credits to paid channels (in-sample attribution).
ml.incremental_revenue.formula_explanation: Total incremental revenue attributed to paid channels by the Bayesian MMM (in-sample attribution, not a forecast); summed from the channel-level decomposition.
ml.incremental_revenue.description: Total incremental revenue attributed to paid channels by the MMM v2 (baseline/organic excluded). summary.total_incremental_revenue, fallback sum of channel-level incremental_revenue in the attribution decomposition. Raw MMM attribution output.

ml.intervention_propensity.title: Mean Propensity Score
ml.intervention_propensity.formula_human: Mean Propensity Score = mean(intervention_propensity) across all scored customers
ml.intervention_propensity.formula_explanation: Average predicted probability across all scored customers that they will order within 90 days if sent an intervention.
ml.intervention_propensity.description: Average predicted probability across scored customers that they will order within 90 days if sent an intervention.

ml.loyalist_likely_pool.title: Top-Decile Pool (Loyalist Likely)
ml.loyalist_likely_pool.formula_explanation: Number of first-time buyers in the highest repeat-propensity segment ('loyalist_likely') — those most likely to become repeat customers. Counted over the repeat_segment column.
ml.loyalist_likely_pool.description: Count of first-time buyers in the highest repeat-propensity segment ('loyalist_likely'), counted over the repeat_segment column (segment derived from repeat_propensity).

ml.markdown_ready_now.title: Markdown-Ready Now
ml.markdown_ready_now.formula_human: Markdown-Ready Now = count of products whose model-assigned markdown_urgency == 'Now' in the product_markdowns artifact.
ml.markdown_ready_now.formula_explanation: Products the model flags for immediate markdown — the items where acting now optimises margin recovery.
ml.markdown_ready_now.description: Number of products the markdown-optimal-timing model flags for immediate markdown (urgency = 'Now') — items where acting now optimises margin recovery. A COUNT over the per-product model output.

ml.median_confidence_interval_width.title: Typical Prediction Range
ml.median_confidence_interval_width.formula_explanation: The median width of the prediction confidence interval, in dollars — the typical +/- band around a predicted order value. Read from the summary; falls back to the median of confidence_interval_width over scored customers.
ml.median_confidence_interval_width.description: Median width of the order-value prediction confidence interval (in dollars) across scored customers; from summary, fallback to median of confidence_interval_width.

ml.message_affinity_avg_confidence.title: Average Confidence
ml.message_affinity_avg_confidence.formula_human: mean(confidence) of each customer's winning message archetype (softmax prob)
ml.message_affinity_avg_confidence.formula_explanation: Average certainty of the message-affinity predictions. Higher means the model distinguishes message preferences more cleanly.
ml.message_affinity_avg_confidence.description: Mean predicted softmax confidence of each customer's winning message archetype across all scored customers.

ml.message_affinity_customers_segmented.title: Total Customers Segmented
ml.message_affinity_customers_segmented.formula_human: len(customer_predictions) — customers assigned a preferred message archetype
ml.message_affinity_customers_segmented.formula_explanation: Total number of customers the message-affinity model assigned a preferred message archetype to. The denominator for all message shares.
ml.message_affinity_customers_segmented.description: Total number of customers the message-affinity model assigned a preferred message archetype to in the latest run.

ml.message_affinity_high_confidence_pool.title: High-Confidence Pool
ml.message_affinity_high_confidence_pool.formula_explanation: Number of customers where the model is confident about the preferred message type. Personalised messaging is safest for this group.
ml.message_affinity_high_confidence_pool.description: Count of scored customers whose winning message archetype scored above 50% predicted probability (the confidence field).

ml.message_affinity_top_message_type.title: Top Message Type
ml.message_affinity_top_message_type.formula_explanation: The single message archetype (Promotional / Educational / Seasonal / Triggered) most customers prefer. Your default content angle.
