ml.message_affinity_top_message_type.description: The most common predicted preferred message archetype (Promotional / Educational / Seasonal / Triggered) across all scored customers.

ml.model_auc.title: Model AUC
ml.model_auc.formula_human: Area under the ROC curve on the held-out test set (shared label across high-value-propensity and intervention-propensity).
ml.model_auc.formula_explanation: Measures how well the model separates responders from non-responders. 71.8% means the model ranks a random responder above a random non-responder 71.8% of the time.
ml.model_auc.description: Holdout ranking quality. On ml-high-value-propensity: summary.model_auc / model_config.test_metrics.auc. On ml-intervention-propensity: metrics.test_auc x 100. Raw training artifact, no derivation.

ml.off_peak_revenue_at_risk.title: Off-Peak Revenue at Risk
ml.off_peak_revenue_at_risk.formula_explanation: Total revenue earned in each category's weakest (trough) month, summed across categories — the soft-season revenue most exposed without intervention.
ml.off_peak_revenue_at_risk.description: Sum of each category's weakest-month (trough) revenue across all categories — the soft-season revenue most exposed without intervention.

ml.offer_high_priority_pool.title: High-Priority Pool
ml.offer_high_priority_pool.formula_explanation: Number of customers ranked into the highest-priority tier for offers at the chosen horizon. Your prime targeting audience.
ml.offer_high_priority_pool.description: Customers ranked into the highest-priority offer tier at the selected horizon - the prime targeting audience.

ml.offer_high_priority_share.title: High-Priority Share
ml.offer_high_priority_share.formula_explanation: Percentage of all scored customers that fall in the high-priority tier. A higher share means value is spread; a lower share means it's concentrated.
ml.offer_high_priority_share.source_code_ref: backend/app/features/offer_value_lift_predictions/ml_service.py:174-184 (high_priority_pool over n_customers); priority breakdown pct at get_priority_breakdown
ml.offer_high_priority_share.description: Percentage of all scored customers in the high-priority tier; higher share = value spread, lower = concentrated.

ml.offer_lift_customers_scored.title: Customers Scored
ml.offer_lift_customers_scored.formula_explanation: Total population the response-score ranker scored for the selected horizon. The denominator behind the priority pool and share.
ml.offer_lift_customers_scored.description: Total population the response-score ranker scored for the selected horizon; the denominator behind the priority pool and share.

ml.offer_segment_match_rate.title: Offer-Segment Match Rate
ml.offer_segment_match_rate.formula_human: Offer-Segment Match Rate = count(cells with recommended_match==true) / total segment-offer cells
ml.offer_segment_match_rate.formula_explanation: Share of customer segments that have a clearly recommended best offer type. Higher means offers can be confidently tailored by segment.
ml.offer_segment_match_rate.source_code_ref: backend/app/features/offer_type_response_predictions/ml_service.py:170-172 (matched = sum(1 for r in seg_list if r['recommended_match']); rate = matched/len(seg_list))
ml.offer_segment_match_rate.description: Share of segment-offer cells flagged as a recommended match in the segment heatmap; higher means offers can be confidently tailored by segment.

ml.offer_top_decile_concentration.title: Top-Decile Concentration
ml.offer_top_decile_concentration.formula_human: Top-Decile Concentration = top_decile_mean_score / overall mean response score
ml.offer_top_decile_concentration.formula_explanation: How many times stronger the top-decile customers' response scores are versus the average. A direct read of how well the model concentrates value.
ml.offer_top_decile_concentration.description: How many times stronger the top-decile customers' response scores are versus the average; a direct read of how well the model concentrates value.

ml.optimal_budget_top_channel.title: Optimal Budget — Top Channel
ml.optimal_budget_top_channel.formula_human: Largest recommended_budget across channels in the model's optimal allocation.
ml.optimal_budget_top_channel.formula_explanation: The recommended budget for the single channel slated to receive the most spend under the model's optimal allocation.
ml.optimal_budget_top_channel.description: Recommended budget for the single channel receiving the most spend. max(channels, key=recommended_budget) from budget_recommendation. ml.recommended_budget is the per-channel optimizer output.

ml.optimal_discount_range.title: Optimal Discount Range
ml.optimal_discount_range.formula_human: Optimal Discount Range = the model's optimal_discount_range metadata if present, else the revenue-maximizing discount +/- 2.5 percentage points.
ml.optimal_discount_range.formula_explanation: The discount-depth band the model expects to maximise net revenue. Stay inside it to balance response uplift against margin given away.
ml.optimal_discount_range.description: The discount-depth band the discount-response model expects to maximise net revenue. Taken from the revenue_impact metadata when available, otherwise built as a +/-2.5pp window around the revenue-maximizing discount.

ml.organic_revenue.title: Organic Revenue
ml.organic_revenue.formula_human: Organic Revenue = max(0, total revenue - paid attributed revenue) — baseline revenue not driven by paid media.
ml.organic_revenue.formula_explanation: Baseline revenue not driven by paid media (brand demand, repeat orders) — total revenue less the model's paid-attributed revenue.
ml.organic_revenue.source_code_ref: backend/app/features/ml_marketing_mix/service.py:328,333 (get_executive_kpis: organic_revenue = total_revenue - total_paid_revenue; returned as max(0, organic_revenue))
ml.organic_revenue.description: Baseline revenue not attributed to paid media (brand demand, repeat orders): total revenue less the marketing-mix model's paid-attributed revenue, floored at zero.

ml.peak_response_hour.title: Peak Response Hour
ml.peak_response_hour.formula_human: Mode of each customer's predicted best_hour (0-23) across customers.
ml.peak_response_hour.formula_explanation: The single hour (0-23) when the largest share of customers are predicted to engage. Your default send time.
ml.peak_response_hour.description: Most common predicted best-response hour. df['best_hour'].mode() over the per-customer timing predictions. Aggregation of a raw model output column.

ml.peak_response_rate.title: Peak Response Rate
ml.peak_response_rate.formula_human: Peak Response Rate = the maximum response_rate across all rows of the discount-response model's response_curves.
ml.peak_response_rate.formula_explanation: The highest predicted response rate achievable at any discount depth. The ceiling on how much a promotion can move customers.
ml.peak_response_rate.description: The highest predicted response rate achievable at any discount depth, from the discount-response model's response_curves artifact — the ceiling on how much a promotion can move customers. A MAX over the model's per-depth response_rate output.

ml.peak_season_category.title: Peak Season Category
ml.peak_season_category.formula_human: category whose max monthly seasonality index is the largest across all categories
ml.peak_season_category.formula_explanation: The category with the single strongest seasonal peak — the one whose best month most exceeds its yearly average.
ml.peak_season_category.description: The category whose single highest monthly seasonality index is the largest across all categories (strongest seasonal peak).

ml.peak_week.title: Peak Week
ml.peak_week.formula_human: max(forecast_revenue) across the 13-week horizon (with its week_start)
ml.peak_week.formula_explanation: Highest single-week point forecast across the 13-week horizon, taken from the weekly forecast series along with the week it falls in.
ml.peak_week.description: Largest single-week point forecast across the 13-week horizon, taken from the weekly forecast series along with the week it falls in.

ml.personalization_opportunity.title: Personalization Opportunity
ml.personalization_opportunity.formula_human: Count of customers flagged high_confidence_timing (confident, distinct best send time).
ml.personalization_opportunity.formula_explanation: Number of customers where the model is confident about their individual best send time, so per-customer timing personalisation will pay off.
ml.personalization_opportunity.description: Customers where the model is confident about individual best send time. df['high_confidence_timing'].sum() (fallback timing_confidence=='high'). ml.high_confidence_timing is the per-customer model output flag.

ml.predicted_new_high_value_customers.title: Predicted New High-Value
ml.predicted_new_high_value_customers.formula_human: Count of customers the model expects to cross into high-value status.
ml.predicted_new_high_value_customers.formula_explanation: Count of customers the model expects to cross into high-value status. The forward-looking growth number for your top tier.
ml.predicted_new_high_value_customers.description: Forward-looking growth number for the top tier. summary.predicted_new_hv_count over the prob_high_value model output. ml.high_value_propensity is the per-customer model output.

ml.premium_tier_count.title: High-Value Targets
ml.premium_tier_count.formula_human: tier_breakdown['Premium'] (count of customers placed in the Premium value tier)
ml.premium_tier_count.formula_explanation: Number of customers the model places in the Premium value tier, read from the summary's tier_breakdown map.
ml.premium_tier_count.description: Count of customers the order-value model places in the Premium value tier, read from summary.tier_breakdown (tier derived from predicted order value).

ml.price_affinity_avg_wtp_score.title: Avg WTP Score
ml.price_affinity_avg_wtp_score.formula_human: Mean predicted willingness-to-pay (wtp_score, 0-1) across all scored customers.
ml.price_affinity_avg_wtp_score.formula_explanation: Average willingness-to-pay score (0-1) across all scored customers — a portfolio-wide read on pricing headroom.
ml.price_affinity_avg_wtp_score.description: Portfolio-wide read on pricing headroom. df['wtp_score'].mean(). Aggregation of the raw per-customer WTP output.

ml.price_affinity_budget_customers.title: Budget Customers
ml.price_affinity_budget_customers.formula_human: Count of scored customers assigned to the Budget price tier.
ml.price_affinity_budget_customers.formula_explanation: Customers in the Budget tier who are highly price-sensitive.
ml.price_affinity_budget_customers.description: Highly price-sensitive customers. (df['price_tier'] == 'Budget').sum(). Category count over the raw per-customer price_tier output.

ml.price_affinity_premium_customers.title: Premium Customers
ml.price_affinity_premium_customers.formula_human: Count of scored customers assigned to the Premium price tier.
ml.price_affinity_premium_customers.formula_explanation: Customers classified into the Premium price tier — those with high willingness-to-pay.
ml.price_affinity_premium_customers.description: High willingness-to-pay customers. (df['price_tier'] == 'Premium').sum(). Category count over the raw per-customer price_tier output.

ml.price_affinity_value_seekers.title: Value Seekers
ml.price_affinity_value_seekers.formula_human: Count of scored customers assigned to the Value price tier.
ml.price_affinity_value_seekers.formula_explanation: Customers in the Value price tier who balance quality against price.
ml.price_affinity_value_seekers.description: Customers balancing quality against price. (df['price_tier'] == 'Value').sum(). Category count over the raw per-customer price_tier output.

ml.price_elasticity_avg_coefficient.title: Avg Elasticity Coefficient
ml.price_elasticity_avg_coefficient.formula_explanation: Portfolio-wide average price elasticity. Values below -1 indicate broadly elastic (price-sensitive) demand.
ml.price_elasticity_avg_coefficient.description: Portfolio-wide average predicted price-elasticity coefficient across all scored products (values below -1 indicate broadly price-sensitive demand).

ml.price_elasticity_highly_elastic_produ.title: Highly Elastic Products
ml.price_elasticity_highly_elastic_produ.formula_explanation: Products where a small price change causes a large demand shift (elasticity below -1.0).
ml.price_elasticity_highly_elastic_produ.description: Count of products whose predicted elasticity coefficient is below -1.0 (a small price change moves demand sharply).

ml.price_elasticity_inelastic_products.title: Inelastic Products
ml.price_elasticity_inelastic_products.formula_explanation: Products where demand is relatively insensitive to price (elasticity between -1.0 and 0).
ml.price_elasticity_inelastic_products.description: Count of products whose predicted elasticity coefficient is between -1.0 and 0 (demand relatively insensitive to price).

ml.price_elasticity_revenue_sensitivity_.title: Revenue Sensitivity Score
ml.price_elasticity_revenue_sensitivity_.formula_explanation: A revenue-weighted average of absolute elasticity — how sensitive total revenue is to price changes, emphasising high-revenue products.
ml.price_elasticity_revenue_sensitivity_.description: Revenue-weighted average of absolute elasticity — how exposed total revenue is to price changes, emphasising high-revenue products.

ml.priority_pool.title: High-Propensity Pool
ml.priority_pool.formula_explanation: Number of prospects the model places in the top 'priority' activation tier — the most likely first-buyers. Read from the summary's by_tier breakdown, with a fallback count over the activation_segment column.
ml.priority_pool.description: Prospects the model places in the top 'priority' activation tier (highest first-purchase propensity).

ml.product_affinity_customers_scored.title: Customers Scored
ml.product_affinity_customers_scored.formula_human: Customers Scored = number of rows in the product-affinity model's customers.parquet (one row per scored customer).
ml.product_affinity_customers_scored.formula_explanation: Total number of customers who received a product affinity recommendation in the latest scoring run (the row count of the scored-customer artifact).
ml.product_affinity_customers_scored.description: Total customers who received a product-affinity recommendation in the latest scoring run — the row count of the scored-customer parquet artifact. A leaf COUNT of model output rows.

ml.product_affinity_high_affinity_pool.title: High Affinity Pool
ml.product_affinity_high_affinity_pool.formula_human: High Affinity Pool = count of scored customers whose model confidence_tier == 'High'.
ml.product_affinity_high_affinity_pool.formula_explanation: Count of customers whose top product recommendation carries a High confidence tier — the most reliable cross-sell audience.
ml.product_affinity_high_affinity_pool.description: Customers whose top product recommendation carries a High confidence tier from the product-affinity model — the most reliable cross-sell audience. A filtered COUNT (confidence_tier == High) over the same scored-customer set as Customers Scored.

ml.product_affinity_hit_rate_at_3.title: Hit Rate @3
ml.product_affinity_hit_rate_at_3.formula_human: Hit Rate @3 = the avg_hit_rate_at_3 value read directly from the product-affinity model_meta artifact.
ml.product_affinity_hit_rate_at_3.formula_explanation: Model-quality metric read from the training artifact: the fraction of customers whose actually-purchased product appeared among the model's top-3 recommendations.
ml.product_affinity_hit_rate_at_3.description: Offline-evaluation quality metric read from the product-affinity training artifact: the fraction of customers whose actually-purchased product appeared among the model's top-3 recommendations. A leaf metadata value, not recomputed at request time.

ml.product_affinity_top_recommended_prod.title: Top Recommended Product
ml.product_affinity_top_recommended_prod.formula_human: Top Recommended Product = the statistical mode of the top_product column across all scored customers (the most frequently predicted top-1 product).
ml.product_affinity_top_recommended_prod.formula_explanation: The single product recommended most frequently across all scored customers (the most common top-1 product).
ml.product_affinity_top_recommended_prod.description: The single product the product-affinity model recommends most often as a customer's top-1 product across all scored customers. A MODE over the model's per-customer top_product output.

ml.product_revenue_forecast.title: Forecast Revenue (next 4w)
ml.product_revenue_forecast.formula_human: Forecast Revenue (next 4w) = sum(product_revenue_forecast_lgbm_score) across products x weeks (summary.total_forecast_revenue)
ml.product_revenue_forecast.formula_explanation: Adds up the LightGBM-predicted revenue across every product for the next four weeks (t+1..t+4). Read from the precomputed summary.json (total_forecast_revenue); the fallback sums the model score column directly.
ml.product_revenue_forecast.description: Sum of the LightGBM-predicted revenue across every product over the next four weeks; from summary.total_forecast_revenue, fallback sums the model score column.

ml.products_forecast.title: Products Forecast
ml.products_forecast.formula_human: n_products = nunique(entity) — distinct products with a next-4-week forecast
ml.products_forecast.formula_explanation: Counts the number of distinct products that have a next-4-week units forecast. Read from summary.json (n_products); the fallback computes df['entity'].nunique().
ml.products_forecast.description: Count of distinct products that have a next-4-week revenue/units forecast; from summary.json (n_products), fallback df['entity'].nunique(). Shared by demand and revenue forecast dashboards.

ml.products_forecasted.title: Products Forecasted
ml.products_forecasted.formula_human: Count of products the demand model produced a forecast for this run.
ml.products_forecasted.formula_explanation: Number of products the demand model produced a forecast for in this run.
ml.products_forecasted.description: Number of forecasted products. len(product_forecasts). Raw scoring count.

ml.projected_incremental_revenue.title: Projected Incremental Revenue
ml.projected_incremental_revenue.formula_human: Additional revenue expected from reallocating spend to the recommended budget mix vs current allocation.
ml.projected_incremental_revenue.formula_explanation: Additional revenue the model expects if you reallocate spend to its recommended budget mix versus the current allocation.
ml.projected_incremental_revenue.description: Pass-through of budget_recommendation.projected_incremental_revenue from the channel saturation/optimizer model. Raw optimizer output, no backend derivation.

ml.projected_margin_recovery.title: Projected Margin Recovery
ml.projected_margin_recovery.formula_human: Projected Margin Recovery = sum of the model's projected_margin_recovery over only the products whose markdown_urgency == 'Now'.
ml.projected_margin_recovery.formula_explanation: Total margin the model projects recovering by acting on the markdown-ready-now products at their optimal timing.
ml.projected_margin_recovery.description: Total margin the markdown-optimal-timing model projects recovering by acting on the markdown-ready-now products at their optimal timing. Sums the per-product projected_margin_recovery field across the same 'Now'-urgency set counted by markdown_ready_now.

ml.quarter_range.title: Quarter Range (80% PI)
ml.quarter_range.formula_explanation: 80% prediction interval for total quarterly revenue: the summed lower and upper weekly bounds (pi80_low / pi80_high) from the forecast. Actual revenue should fall inside this range about 8 times in 10.
ml.quarter_range.description: 80% prediction interval for total quarterly revenue: the summed weekly lower and upper bounds (pi80_low / pi80_high) over the 13-week horizon.

ml.quarter_total_forecast.title: Forecast Quarter Total
ml.quarter_total_forecast.formula_explanation: Point-forecast sum of weekly revenue across the next 13 weeks, read from the forecast summary artifact (sum of forecast_revenue over the horizon).
ml.quarter_total_forecast.description: Point-forecast sum of weekly revenue across the next 13 weeks, read from the forecast summary (sum of forecast_revenue over the horizon).

ml.reallocation_upside.title: Reallocation Upside
ml.reallocation_upside.formula_explanation: Extra revenue available from shifting the same total budget toward higher-marginal-return channels, with no added spend; the optimizer's expected daily response over the current daily response, expressed as a percent gain.
ml.reallocation_upside.description: Extra revenue from shifting the same total budget toward higher-marginal-return channels. Computed in the frontend ReallocationUpsideMetric from budget_optimization.json (optimized_daily_response, current_daily_response). ml.current_daily_response and ml.optimized_daily_response are MMM optimizer output fields.

ml.repeat_propensity.title: Avg Repeat Propensity
ml.repeat_propensity.formula_human: summary.score_mean, else mean(repeat_propensity) over scored first-time buyers
ml.repeat_propensity.formula_explanation: The average predicted probability that a first-time buyer makes a repeat purchase, read from the summary's score_mean with a fallback to the mean of the repeat_propensity column.
ml.repeat_propensity.description: Average predicted probability that a first-time buyer makes a repeat purchase, from summary.score_mean with a fallback to the mean of the repeat_propensity column.

ml.repeat_propensity_median.title: Median Repeat Propensity
ml.repeat_propensity_median.formula_human: summary.score_median — midpoint of the repeat-propensity distribution
ml.repeat_propensity_median.formula_explanation: The median repeat-purchase propensity across scored first-time buyers — half score higher, half lower — read from the summary's score_median field.
ml.repeat_propensity_median.description: Median repeat-purchase propensity across scored first-time buyers (half score higher, half lower), from summary.score_median.

ml.retained_revenue.title: Retained Revenue Forecast
ml.retained_revenue.formula_human: Retained Revenue Forecast = (1 - avg_churn_probability) x avg_pred_clv_180d x total_customers_scored, shown as retained share of total predicted revenue
ml.retained_revenue.formula_explanation: The probability-weighted revenue we expect to keep over the horizon — same idea as ERV, presented as a 'retained share' progress bar to mirror Revenue at Risk.
ml.retained_revenue.description: Probability-weighted revenue expected to be kept over the horizon - same computation as ERV, presented as a retained-share progress bar.

ml.retention_rate.title: Retention Rate
ml.retention_rate.formula_human: Retention Rate = 1 - churn_rate (share of customers expected to stay over the horizon)
ml.retention_rate.formula_explanation: Inverse of churn rate — share of customers expected to stay over the horizon.
ml.retention_rate.source_code_ref: backend/app/features/churn_analysis/ml_service.py:294-310 (retention is complement of high_risk_pct/churn_rate); registry depends_on churn_rate+churn_probability
ml.retention_rate.description: Inverse of churn rate - share of customers expected to stay over the horizon.

ml.rev_max_discount.title: Revenue-Maximizing Discount
ml.rev_max_discount.formula_human: Revenue-Maximizing Discount = the discount_pct of the revenue_impact row with the highest net_revenue (uplift minus discount cost).
ml.rev_max_discount.formula_explanation: The single discount depth that produces the highest net revenue (uplift minus discount cost). The profit-optimal promo depth.
ml.rev_max_discount.description: The single discount depth that produces the highest net revenue in the discount-response model's revenue_impact curve — the profit-optimal promo depth (argmax of net_revenue over discount_pct).

ml.revenue_at_stockout_risk.title: Revenue at Stockout Risk
ml.revenue_at_stockout_risk.formula_human: Sum of revenue_at_risk across all Critical/High-risk products.
ml.revenue_at_stockout_risk.formula_explanation: Total revenue exposed across all Critical/High-risk products — the sales at stake if stock-outs are not prevented.
ml.revenue_at_stockout_risk.description: Total revenue exposed across high-risk products. Sums revenue_at_risk for rows with risk_level in {Critical,High} in get_dashboard_summary.

ml.revenue_forecast_avg_per_product.title: Avg / Product
ml.revenue_forecast_avg_per_product.formula_explanation: Total forecast revenue divided by the number of forecast products, giving the mean predicted revenue per product. Read from summary.json (mean_forecast_per_product).
ml.revenue_forecast_avg_per_product.description: Total forecast revenue divided by number of forecast products; mean predicted revenue per product (summary.json mean_forecast_per_product).

ml.revenue_forecast_top_product.title: Top Product
ml.revenue_forecast_top_product.formula_human: Top Product = argmax_product(forecast_revenue); Share = top product's forecast_revenue / total forecast_revenue
ml.revenue_forecast_top_product.formula_explanation: Identifies the single product with the highest forecast revenue and shows its share of the total. Computed client-side by forecastStats() over the forecast points (top_value, top_share).
ml.revenue_forecast_top_product.source_code_ref: backend/app/features/ml_product_revenue_forecast/service.py:179-197 (per-product forecast points); top/share in dashboard-v2 forecastStats()
ml.revenue_forecast_top_product.description: The product with the highest forecast revenue and its share of the total; computed client-side by forecastStats() (top_value, top_share).

ml.revenue_per_top_offer.title: Revenue per Top Offer
ml.revenue_per_top_offer.formula_explanation: The revenue attributable to the best-converting offer type. Confirms whether high conversion also translates into dollars.
ml.revenue_per_top_offer.description: Revenue attributable to the top-converting offer type; confirms whether high conversion translates into dollars.

ml.revenue_protected_customers.title: Revenue Protected
ml.revenue_protected_customers.formula_human: Count of customers predicted NOT to need a discount to convert.
ml.revenue_protected_customers.formula_explanation: Number of customers predicted NOT to need a discount to convert. Discounting them just gives away margin, so the model recommends protecting that revenue.
ml.revenue_protected_customers.description: Customers who would buy at full price, so excluded from discounting (margin protected). summary.revenue_protected_count, the complement of discount-responsive over prob_discount_response. ml.discount_response_probability is the per-customer model output.

ml.revenue_uplift_vs_no_markdown.title: Revenue Uplift vs No Markdown
ml.revenue_uplift_vs_no_markdown.formula_human: Revenue Uplift vs No Markdown = the revenue_uplift_vs_no_markdown value read directly from the model_config training artifact.
ml.revenue_uplift_vs_no_markdown.formula_explanation: Model-estimated revenue gain from following the optimal markdown plan versus taking no markdown action, read from the training artifact.
ml.revenue_uplift_vs_no_markdown.description: Model-estimated revenue gain from following the optimal markdown plan versus taking no markdown action, read verbatim from the markdown model's model_config artifact (training metadata). A leaf metadata value, not recomputed.

ml.seasonal_sensitivity.title: Seasonal Sensitivity
ml.seasonal_sensitivity.formula_explanation: How much customer response swings with the season, on average. Higher means timing campaigns to seasonal peaks matters more.
ml.seasonal_sensitivity.description: Average seasonal responsiveness across customers. df['seasonal_sensitivity_index'].mean(). ml.seasonal_sensitivity_index is the per-customer model output column.

ml.stable_high_value_customers.title: Stable High-Value
ml.stable_high_value_customers.formula_human: Count of High/VIP/Whale segment customers whose predicted trajectory class is 'hold'.
ml.stable_high_value_customers.formula_explanation: Count of top-tier customers predicted to hold their value (neither rising nor falling). This is your dependable revenue core.
ml.stable_high_value_customers.description: Top-tier customers predicted to hold their value. Mask (predicted_trajectory_<h> == 'hold') & current_segment in {High,VIP,Whale} over the parquet (_stable_high_value_count).

ml.stage_transition_rate.title: Stage Transition Rate
ml.stage_transition_rate.formula_human: (customers predicted to improve stage + customers predicted to decline stage) / total customers scored.
ml.stage_transition_rate.formula_explanation: Fraction of all scored customers whose lifecycle stage is predicted to change (either improving or declining). Higher values mean a more dynamic base.
ml.stage_transition_rate.description: Fraction of scored customers whose lifecycle stage is predicted to change. (transition_counts.improve + transition_counts.decline) / total_customers_scored, computed in get_dashboard_summary.

ml.top_converting_offer.title: Top Converting Offer
ml.top_converting_offer.formula_explanation: The offer type that converts at the highest rate. Your strongest default promotional mechanic.
ml.top_converting_offer.description: The offer type with the highest predicted conversion rate; the strongest default promotional mechanic.

ml.top_growth_driver.title: Top Growth Driver
ml.top_growth_driver.formula_human: Top Growth Driver = humanize(feature_importance[0].feature) (highest-gain feature in grow-probability model)
ml.top_growth_driver.formula_explanation: The single feature that most influences the grow-probability model, taken as the top-ranked entry by gain in the feature-importance artifact and formatted for display.
ml.top_growth_driver.description: The single feature most influencing the grow-probability model; top-ranked entry by gain in the feature-importance artifact, formatted for display.

ml.top_intervention_type.title: Top Intervention Type
ml.top_intervention_type.formula_explanation: The intervention trigger category with the highest customer count: Abandoned Browse, Checkout, or Cart.
ml.top_intervention_type.description: The intervention trigger category (Abandoned Browse / Checkout / Cart) with the highest customer count across scored customers.

ml.total_expected_revenue.title: Forecast Revenue
ml.total_expected_revenue.formula_explanation: Total dollars the model expects from every scored customer's next order combined. Read from the dashboard summary's total_expected_next_cycle_revenue field.
ml.total_expected_revenue.description: Total dollars the order-value model expects from every scored customer's next order combined, from summary.total_expected_next_cycle_revenue.

ml.total_prospects_scored.title: Total Prospects
ml.total_prospects_scored.formula_explanation: How many prospects (customers who have not yet made a first purchase) the activation model scored this run. Read from the summary's total_prospects field, falling back to the scored-customers row count.
ml.total_prospects_scored.description: Count of prospects (not-yet-first-purchase customers) scored by the activation model this run.

ml.total_recommended_budget.title: Total Recommended Budget
ml.total_recommended_budget.formula_human: Sum of recommended_budget across all channels under the model's optimal allocation.
ml.total_recommended_budget.formula_explanation: The total spend the model recommends across all channels under its optimal allocation. The envelope the per-channel recommendations sum to.
ml.total_recommended_budget.description: Total spend the model recommends across channels. budget_recommendation.total_recommended_budget, fallback sum of per-channel recommended_budget. ml.recommended_budget is the per-channel optimizer output.

ml.total_revenue.title: Total Revenue
ml.total_revenue.formula_human: Total Revenue = paid (attributed) revenue / (1 - organic_revenue_pct/100) — the model's paid revenue grossed up by its organic share so paid + organic sum to the whole window.
ml.total_revenue.formula_explanation: Total revenue over the modelled window: paid (attributed) revenue grossed up by the model's organic share so paid + organic sum to the whole.
ml.total_revenue.source_code_ref: backend/app/features/ml_marketing_mix/service.py:324-327 (get_executive_kpis: organic_pct from dashboard_summary.json; total_revenue = total_paid_revenue / (1 - organic_pct))
ml.total_revenue.description: Total revenue over the modelled window: the marketing-mix model's attributed paid revenue grossed up by the organic-revenue share read from the model's dashboard_summary artifact.

ml.trajectory_customers_scored.title: Customers Scored
ml.trajectory_customers_scored.formula_human: Total number of customers the value-trajectory model scored this run.
ml.trajectory_customers_scored.formula_explanation: Total number of customers the value-trajectory model scored in this run. The denominator for every share metric on the page.
ml.trajectory_customers_scored.description: Denominator for trajectory share metrics. summary.n_customers_total or len(customers.parquet). Raw scoring count.

ml.trajectory_model_accuracy.title: Model Accuracy
ml.trajectory_model_accuracy.formula_human: Top-1 classification accuracy of the trajectory model on the holdout set.
ml.trajectory_model_accuracy.formula_explanation: How often the model's predicted trajectory class matched the actual outcome on held-out data. Higher means more trustworthy predictions.
ml.trajectory_model_accuracy.description: Model-quality metric read from model_meta.test_metrics (top1, fallback adj_acc). Raw training artifact, no derivation.

ml.untapped_low_spend.title: Untapped Low-Spenders
ml.untapped_low_spend.formula_explanation: Count of customers in the lowest historical spend tier who are flagged as likely to grow — the under-monetised pool with the most upside. Read from the flagged_count of the smallest AOV-tier bucket in the model summary.
ml.untapped_low_spend.description: Count of growth-flagged customers in the lowest historical AOV (spend) tier - the under-monetised pool with the most upside.

ml.watch_pool.title: Watch Pool
ml.watch_pool.formula_explanation: Number of prospects in the middle 'watch' activation tier — moderate first-purchase likelihood worth nurturing rather than pushing hard. Read from the summary's by_tier breakdown.
ml.watch_pool.description: Prospects in the middle 'watch' activation tier (moderate first-purchase likelihood) worth nurturing rather than pushing hard.

ml.whale_target_count.title: Top Spenders
ml.whale_target_count.formula_human: summary.high_value_predicted_orders_count — customers flagged as whale targets
ml.whale_target_count.formula_explanation: Number of customers flagged as whale targets — those whose predicted order value places them among the very top spenders. Read from the summary's high_value_predicted_orders_count field.
ml.whale_target_count.description: Count of customers flagged as whale targets (predicted order value among the very top spenders), from summary.high_value_predicted_orders_count.
